"http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "A 122-year-old wooden railroad bridge in Bellevue, Washington, is getting a new life—and a new purpose.The Wilburton Trestle, a 975-foot-long, 102-foot-tall structure originally built in 1904 for the Northern Pacific Railway, is being reimagined as a community asset that connects housing, jobs, and transit across the Eastside and transformed into a centerpiece of the Eastrail regional trail. When it opens in summer 2027, visitors will walk or roll along an 18-foot-wide trail deck with three viewing platforms offering panoramic views of the Bellevue skyline, Lake Washington, and the Olympic Mountains.Amazon is helping to make it happen. In September 2021, the company committed $7.5 million to the project—$5 million for the Wilburton Trestle and $2.5 million for the adjacent Wilburton Trail Segment—closing the funding gap needed to begin construction.\"Amazon is proud to partner with King County on this iconic project, transforming a unique piece of our regional history into a permanent community asset for everyone,\" said David Zapolsky, Amazon's chief global affairs and legal officer. \"Strong public-private partnerships make projects like the Wilburton Trestle possible, and make our region a great place to live, work, and visit. We can't wait for this part of Eastrail to open and serve generations to come.\"King County Executive Girmay Zahilay recently led a hard-hat tour of the trestle alongside Zapolsky, King County Councilmember Claudia Balducci, Bellevue Deputy Mayor Dave Hamilton, and other regional partners to mark the project's progress.Amazon's investment in the Wilburton Trestle reflects the company's commitment to the communities where its employees live and work. From preserving regional history to supporting multimodal transit infrastructure, projects like these help make the Puget Sound region a great place to call home for generations to come.", "dateModified": "2026-04-13T18:39:46Z", "datePublished": "2026-04-13T18:39:46Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/025a1c4/2147483647/strip/false/crop/5268x2963+1+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F79%2F3a%2Ff22b78d645f9ab3e64126297e9df%2Fdo-parks-wilburton-trestle-20260408-27.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon's $7.5 million investment helps transform a 122-year-old railroad bridge into a trail for everyone", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazons-7-5-million-investment-helps-transform-a-122-year-old-railroad-bridge-into-a-trail-for-everyone"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon celebrated a major milestone in regional transportation as the Crosslake Connection opened, bringing the world's first light rail system operating on a floating bridge to the Seattle-Bellevue area. The company served as presenting sponsor for the Transportation Choices Coalition's reception marking this historic achievement on March 27.Supporting sustainable transportation in the Puget Sound\"We know that jobs and great workspaces matter, but so does how people get to them,\" said David Zapolsky, chief global affairs and legal officer at Amazon, highlighting the company's commitment to sustainable transportation infrastructure. \"We are committed to ensuring our growth is sustainable and that we are investing in the right transportation infrastructure for this community. Public transit is core to our sustainability goals. It gives people reliable alternatives to driving, reduces congestion, and improves air quality for everyone.\"The new light rail connection is critical for the region's growing workforce. Amazon’s Puget Sound offices are strategically located within walking distance of transit stations, reinforcing the company's dedication to sustainable mobility solutions.$200 million commitment to transit-oriented affordable housingAmazon's transportation commitment extends beyond this celebration. The company has consistently supported regional transit efforts. In 2021, Amazon committed $100 million in partnership with Sound Transit to accelerate the creation of affordable housing units near light rail stations across the Puget Sound region. Last year, Amazon committed an additional $100 million to the City of Bellevue to accelerate affordable housing production, with a focus on transit-oriented sites that give low-to-moderate income families close proximity to community resources.This milestone reflects Amazon's broader philosophy of community partnership and long-term investment in the regions where it operates, demonstrating how public transit infrastructure supports both economic growth and environmental sustainability.", "dateModified": "2026-03-28T22:15:14Z", "datePublished": "2026-03-28T16:04:58Z", "image": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This week, Amazon joined partners across Seattle to celebrate two meaningful milestones in our ongoing effort to create and preserve affordable homes—a groundbreaking in Lower Queen Anne and a ribbon-cutting in the Othello neighborhood.Altaire at Queen Anne: Breaking ground on opportunityOn March 9, the Urban League of Metropolitan Seattle and SRM broke ground on Altaire at Queen Anne, a 114-unit affordable rental housing development in Seattle's Lower Queen Anne neighborhood. The development will offer studio, one-, two-, and three-bedroom apartments for households earning between 40% and 70% of the area median income (AMI)—serving local workers, families, and artists in one of Seattle's higher-cost neighborhoods.For five years, the site sat vacant. Now, through a mission-driven public-private partnership—including more than $9 million from Amazon's housing fund alongside support from the Seattle Office of Housing, the Washington State Housing Finance Commission, Citibank, and PNC Bank—it will be transformed into a place people can call home.Atrium Court: A ribbon-cutting in OthelloThe very next day, on March 10, we celebrated the grand opening of Atrium Court—less than 200 feet from the Othello Light Rail Station. The development includes 200 income-restricted apartments affordable to households earning 50% to 80% of AMI.Amazon's housing fund contributed a $16 million below-market loan to help make this transit-oriented development a reality, alongside partners including Allivate Impact Capital, CEI-Boulos Capital Management, and Trez Capital.\"Working families deserve the stability of knowing their home will remain affordable for generations to come,\" said Senthil Sankaran, managing principal, Amazon's housing fund. \"Our partnership at Atrium Court highlights what's possible when mission-driven partners come together around a shared commitment: 99 years of affordability, right next to transit, in one of Seattle's most vibrant, diverse neighborhoods.\"This week, 385 more Seattle families moved one step closer to a stable, affordable home, all protected by our commitment to 99 years of affordability. That's what this work is all about.", "dateModified": "2026-03-24T22:23:10Z", "datePublished": "2026-03-24T22:23:10Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/0d9464e/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2500x1406+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F4b%2F2d%2F8a84884c4c7693bff484634bb4dc%2Fdsc08005.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "From Queen Anne to Othello: Amazon celebrates new affordable homes this week", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=from-queen-anne-to-othello-amazon-celebrates-new-affordable-homes-this-week"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This February, Amazon was proud to support Lunar New Year celebrations across the Puget Sound region—honoring traditions that bring families together and strengthen the cultural fabric of our communities.Partnering with Friends of Little Saigon for TếtFrom February 6-13, Amazon partnered with Friends of Little Saigon for their Tết Celebration, a series of cultural events for all ages. In Vietnamese culture, Tết symbolizes renewal, family, and cultural identity, offering neighbors a chance to experience Vietnamese traditions through food, art, and community connection.\"Tết is a time to come together, honor our heritage, and welcome the new year with joy,\" said Quynh Pham, executive director of Friends of Little Saigon. \"Partnering with Amazon allowed us to expand our celebration and welcome more neighbors into the heart of Vietnamese culture, helping make our region a place where everyone can participate in the traditions that make our communities whole.\"Celebrating with Seattle Symphony at Benaroya HallOn February 13, Amazon joined the Seattle Symphony Lunar New Year Gala at Benaroya Hall. This special evening brought together AAPI (Asian American and Pacific Islander) leaders and community members with music director Xian Zhang to celebrate the Year of the Horse through the power of music and shared cultural pride.Supporting family celebrations in Kirkland and BellevueOn February 28, Amazon supported two celebrations for Kirkland and Bellevue families. The City of Kirkland's celebration invited the community to explore traditional celebrations through food, crafts, dancing, and cultural demonstrations. That same day, the Emerald Parents Association, in partnership with Amazon, King County, and 4Culture, hosted its Lunar New Year celebration at Factoria Square Mall, featuring cultural exhibitions, a lion dance, food, crafts, games, and hanfu.\"The exhibition created a vibrant, welcoming space for Chinese families in our region to celebrate Lunar New Year close to home—strengthening cultural identity, fostering pride, and deepening community connections,\" said Bev Ni, vice president of outreach at Emerald Parents Association.Amazon is grateful to help celebrate the traditions that make the Puget Sound region vibrant and welcoming for all.", "dateModified": "2026-03-11T19:57:20Z", "datePublished": "2026-03-11T19:57:20Z", "image": "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This January, Amazon teamed up with USO Northwest to support 2,600 U.S. service members deploying to South Korea. Since 1941, the USO has supported service members and their families from enlistment through deployment and their transition home. Amazon has been a proud partner since 2022. USO Northwest operates across Washington, Oregon, and Idaho, serving over 150,000 military members and their families each year.Amazon volunteers assemble care packages for deploying service membersOn January 23, Seattle City Council member Bob Kettle joined more than 60 Amazon volunteers at our Seattle headquarters to assemble care packages and write encouraging letters for service members at Joint Base Lewis-McChord. The care packages will be distributed ahead of 11 flights to South Korea throughout March, providing comfort and connection to service members as they begin their overseas assignments. “Today, we’re grateful for partners like Amazon who help us carry out that mission by showing up in meaningful ways,” said Don Leingang, executive director for USO Northwest. “While it may look like a simple care package, gestures like these send a powerful message to service members: They are seen, valued, and supported by a grateful nation.”Warriors at Amazon leads military support effortsLeading the volunteer effort is the Warriors at Amazon, an employee-led group representing current and former military personnel, their families, and supporters. With more than 20,000 members, Warriors fosters an inclusive community for service members and veterans, helping them transition and thrive in civilian roles. This volunteer event reflects Amazon’s broader commitment to military families and service members. In 2024, Amazon surpassed our goal of hiring 100,000 veterans and military spouses, and our upskilling programs help veterans grow their careers. In the Puget Sound, Amazon supports organizations like the South Sound Military and Communities Partnership, Blue Star Families, and events like Seafair to celebrate those who serve. As thousands of service members head to South Korea, Amazon remains committed to supporting the military community in the Pacific Northwest and beyond.", "dateModified": "2026-02-20T18:08:42Z", "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Two transformative affordable housing projects are moving forward in Seattle's historic neighborhoods, with support from Amazon's housing fund helping local developers preserve community stability and prevent displacement.Revitalizing the historic Eclipse Hotel in Chinatown-International DistrictIn Seattle's Chinatown-International District (CID), the Seattle Chinatown International District Preservation and Development Authority (SCIDpda) acquired and is rehabilitating the historic Eclipse Hotel. Built in 1908, this landmark building has stood at the heart of the community for over a century. While its ground-floor retail spaces remain vibrant and fully occupied, the upper floors have sat vacant since the 1970s. Through a $2 million Amazon grant, SCIDpda will convert these unused spaces into 39 affordable units—specifically designed for seniors earning at or below 40% of area median income (AMI). This mission-driven project ensures that longtime community members can age in the neighborhood they've called home for decades.Preserving affordability at Brighton Apartments in South SeattleIn South Seattle, the Brighton Apartments—a 125-unit community built in 1968—is undergoing critical preservation work. Without intervention, the property faced rising rents and deteriorating conditions. With a $3.5 million grant from Amazon's housing fund, Brighton Communities (formerly known as the South Seattle Senior Foundation) can renovate while preserving affordability for 99 years. All homes will remain affordable for households earning between 30% and 80% of AMI.\"Preserving affordable housing in neighborhoods like the CID and South Seattle ensures that the people who make our city work—first responders, teachers, health care workers—can continue to call these communities home,\" said David Zapolsky, Amazon chief global affairs and legal officer. \"Working alongside mission-driven developers like SCIDpda and Brighton Communities, we're helping ensure our communities remain vibrant and accessible today and for generations to come.\"These projects demonstrate the power of partnership in preserving community stability and ensuring Seattle remains accessible to the service industry employees who make our city thrive.", "dateModified": "2026-02-03T17:30:14Z", "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=affordable-housing-for-longtime-residents-preserved-in-two-seattle-neighborhoods"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon attorney Yousri Omar was named a Seattle Kraken Hero of the Deep for leading legal clinics at Mary's Place for families experiencing homelessness. Omar serves on Mary's Place's board and helped build Amazon's Pro Bono Program. Amazon Legal has provided free legal services to Mary's Place for over 10 years, serving thousands of families.“I’m deeply honored to be recognized as a Hero of the Deep. This work is about showing up for families experiencing homelessness, and I’ve been privileged to do that alongside the incredible team at Mary’s Place,” said Omar. “I’m grateful to Amazon and my legal colleagues, whose commitment to pro bono service has made it possible to help families navigate some of their most difficult moments. This recognition really belongs to all of them.”Amazon's partnership with Mary's PlaceSince 2016, Amazon has donated over $100 million in cash and in-kind support to Mary's Place to address family homelessness in King County. In 2020, they opened a permanent shelter inside an Amazon building on the Seattle campus, serving 200 parents and children nightly and over 1,000 families annually.\"Yousri and Amazon attorneys help parents navigate legal challenges that can make all the difference to secure stable housing,” said Dominique Alex, Mary’s Place chief executive officer. “We're so grateful to Yousri, every attorney who gives their time, and the Seattle Kraken for shining a light on this incredible partnership.\"Amazon employees also support Mary's Place through volunteer initiatives including donation organization, meal preparation, computer classes, and toy drives that have provided more than 5,000 toys for families.Recognition and community impactAs part of the Hero of the Deep honor, Mary's Place will receive a $32,000 grant from the One Roof Foundation.This recognition reflects Amazon's commitment to supporting communities through employee expertise and partnerships that address critical needs like housing insecurity.", "dateModified": "2026-01-29T16:46:38Z", "datePublished": "2026-01-29T16:46:38Z", "image": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "From November 4-11, Amazon proudly joined Operation Green Light for Veterans—a nationwide effort led by the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO) to shine a green light in support of veterans, service members, and their families. Across the country, hundreds of Amazon buildings glowed green as a symbol of gratitude and unity.\"By lighting hundreds of Amazon buildings green across the country for Operation Green Light, we're proud to stand alongside the National Association of Counties in honoring those who have served,\" said Charlotte La Belle, head of Amazon's global military affairs and U.S. Army veteran. \"Veterans bring exceptional leadership, expertise, and dedication to Amazon every day. This initiative reflects our deep commitment to supporting their transition to roles in the private sector and encouraging other organizations to join us in celebrating and supporting our veteran community.\"Across the Puget Sound region, Amazon employees are turning their own lights green at home and volunteering with local veteran-focused organizations, creating a ripple of appreciation that extends beyond the workplace. The Warriors at Amazon employee-led group—representing more than 20,000 members globally—continues to lead by example, championing mentorship, service, and community engagement that strengthens Amazon's culture of belonging. Their dedication reminds us that honoring service isn't just about recognition, it's about action.As Amazon sites across Washington state joined in, Operation Green Light became more than a visual tribute—it became a reminder of Amazon's ongoing commitment to opportunity, belonging, and community for those who've served. Each act of service and each light, from Seattle to Spokane, illuminated what it means to stand united in gratitude.", "dateModified": "2025-11-17T16:51:32Z", "datePublished": "2025-11-17T16:51:32Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/b8fe009/2147483647/strip/false/crop/5712x3213+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F73%2F00%2F08fab92047cfb1bc9bd08d320069%2Fimg-8033-1.JPG", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Honoring service and strength from Seattle", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=honoring-service-and-strength-from-seattle"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon is dedicated to ensuring communities have reliable access to food year-round. As part of our overall commitment, we’ve donated an additional $2.25 million to local food banks in the Puget Sound region to meet increased demand. Throughout 2025, we’ve also used our transportation network to deliver more than 4.5 million meals, on behalf of local food banks, directly to Puget Sound residents and have donated more than 2.8 million meals to local community organizations to help those experiencing hunger in Washington state.\"Food security remains a serious challenge in the Puget Sound region, with recent demand on local food banks reaching record levels,\" said Bettina Stix, global director of community impact at Amazon. \"These donations combined with Amazon’s free home delivery and bulk delivery services offered to food banks strengthen the infrastructure of our local food system and ensure our neighbors have reliable access to nutritious meals.\"The contributions support key regional organizations addressing food insecurity including, Food Lifeline, Snoqualmie Food Bank, Edmonds Food Bank, Northwest Harvest, Emergency Food Network, Bellevue LifeSpring, Hopelink, and Renewal Food Bank. These funds will help provide thousands of families with nutritious meals, holiday food assistance, and year-round hunger relief services.Amazon helps food banks reach more people using our established last-mile infrastructure and logistics expertise. On top of financial donations, our delivery network—including Delivery Service Partners, Amazon Flex, and Amazon Shipping—has delivered more than 60 million meals directly to over 200,000 families experiencing food insecurity across the United States since 2020. We also provide bulk transportation services for free, moving tens of thousands of pounds of food weekly between suppliers, food banks, and community distribution points using our fleet of vehicles. In addition, our grocery stores regularly donate excess food donations to local food banks. Together, these efforts help ensure food reaches families in need regardless of transportation costs, barriers, or mobility challenges.Learn more about Amazon’s commitment to food security.", {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon, in partnership with the Gary Sinise Foundation (GSF), donated more than $100,000 in essential equipment to Seattle-area firefighters in honor of National First Responders Day on October 28.The donation included a $56,000 grant to the Bellevue Fire Foundation for thermal imaging cameras that allow firefighters to see through smoke and darkness, and $50,000 to South King Fire Department in Federal Way for 14 self-contained breathing apparatuses and two night-vision helmets.During the events, Amazon volunteers and local public officials served meals to first responders to thank them for their service and dedication to community safety.\"First responders play an essential role in keeping our communities safe, and we're proud to support the Gary Sinise Foundation to help ensure fire departments across Puget Sound have the equipment and resources they need,\" said Dean Allsopp, community engagement manager for Amazon.With this recent contribution, the total amount of first responder grants provided across Washington state now exceeds $450,000. Previous grantees include Spokane County Fire District 10, Franklin County Fire District, Everett Fire Department, West Pierce Fire and Rescue, and Seattle Fire Department.\"Through our partnership, Amazon has displayed incredible compassion for our mission and a commitment to delivering direct meaningful impact to the heroes of our communities, the first responders who put their lives on the line every day to keep us safe,\" said Chrissy Kreisel, senior director of corporate and foundation relations at the Gary Sinise Foundation.The Gary Sinise Foundation was established in 2011 by actor and humanitarian Gary Sinise, who has supported veterans, first responders, and defenders for more than 40 years. Amazon's three-year partnership with GSF extends beyond Washington state, supporting programs including annual healing retreats for families of fallen heroes and the construction of specially adapted smart homes nationwide.", "dateModified": "2025-10-29T16:00:04Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-29T16:00:04Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/103440e/2147483647/strip/false/crop/5131x2886+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fc7%2F8f%2F588dc9d0452f9ade9b27a8fbdeae%2Fimg-9948.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon and Gary Sinise Foundation donate vital equipment to Puget Sound area firefighters", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazon-and-gary-sinise-foundation-donate-vital-equipment-to-puget-sound-area-firefighters"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Last year, Amazon stepped up and supported the CID Business Improvement Area to bring the Night Market back to the historic neighborhood.Amazon proudly presented the 18th annual Chinatown-International District (CID) Night Market Saturday, September 26, marking the company's second consecutive year supporting the vibrant community celebration. During the event, the historic streets of Seattle's CID were transformed into a lively festival featuring more than 100 local vendors, performers, and thousands of neighbors and community members.\"The Night Market has always held a special place in my heart. Growing up in the Chinatown-International District, I experienced firsthand how these cultural celebrations strengthen the fabric of our community,\" said Taylor Hoang, senior manager for Community Engagement at Amazon. \"This isn't just about one day and one night of festivities, it's about honoring traditions, supporting family-owned businesses, and ensuring this historic neighborhood continues to thrive. That's what being a good neighbor truly means—showing up consistently and contributing meaningfully to the communities where we live and work.\"This year's Night Market overlapped with the Mid-Autumn Moon Festival, a 3,000-year-old tradition celebrated across Asia that marks the end of the summer harvest. The Seattle celebration made its debut in August 2006 as a small gathering organized by the youth leadership group WILD (Wilderness Inner-city Leadership Development) to bring residents, visitors, and vendors into the neighborhood as part of National Night Out. In 2023, the Night Market was canceled due to a lack of sponsorship. The following year, Amazon stepped in with a $250,000 three-year commitment as the lead sponsor.\"The Night Market is a great event because it shows what the neighborhood could look like at its peak,\" said Tuyen Than, executive director of the Chinatown-International District Business Improvement Area. \"And that's what we're going for: a Night Market, but all throughout the year.\"", "dateModified": "2025-09-30T22:34:47Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-30T22:34:47Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/f84fd1c/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1400x788+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F68%2F69%2Fb4c39f114ecd86306869aa072cbd%2Flion-dance-cidbia.png", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Celebrating culture and community at Seattle's 18th annual Chinatown International District (CID) Night Marke", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=celebrating-culture-and-community-at-seattles-18th-annual-chinatown-international-district-cid-night-market"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "The Rainier Valley Food Bank celebrated the grand opening of its newly renovated facility this month, welcoming hundreds of community members to the expanded space. Amazon contributed $1 million to the food bank's capital campaign, helping support its transformation from a small 1,200-square-foot location into a nearly 12,000-square-foot resource center.\"Amazon's generous contribution to Rainier Valley Food Bank directly addresses one of our city's most pressing challenges—ensuring all Seattle residents have access to affordable, nutritious food,\" said Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell. \"As we continue efforts to expand healthy food access, we recognize this investment doesn't just expand a building - it strengthens a vital community resource that provides dignity, nutrition, and hope for thousands of South Seattle residents.\"Amazon's contribution reflects the company's commitment to addressing food insecurity in its hometown communities. Using the same delivery network that serves our retail customers, Amazon partners with food banks like Rainier Valley Food Bank to bring groceries directly to the doorsteps of families in need for free. Since 2020, Amazon has delivered more than 50 million meals across the US and UK, including 4.5 million meals in the Puget Sound region alone.The renovation of Rainier Valley Food Bank not only expands its footprint, allowing for more space to help people in need, but it also creates an elevated shopping experience and offers services beyond only food distribution. This includes:A grocery-store-style shopping area with refrigerated sections, fresh produce, and essential itemsA commercial kitchen for preparing ready-to-go lunches and hot mealsA dedicated resource center providing housing assistance and employment informationOutdoor courtyard and garden space for community gatheringsThe new facility is now open providing essential services to South Seattle residents in need. Learn more about Amazon's commitment to food security and how we're helping fight hunger in our communities.", "dateModified": "2025-09-26T17:12:25Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-26T17:12:25Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/6d84cd5/2147483647/strip/false/crop/6000x3375+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F3b%2Fef%2F161cd40743e7aaa904fc0dbbe060%2Fdsc02860-enhanced-nr.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon celebrates grand opening of Rainier Valley Food Bank's new facility", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazon-celebrates-grand-opening-of-rainier-valley-food-banks-new-facility"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "With support from Amazon, Mary's Place and Mercy Housing Northwest broke ground today on a project that will combine an emergency family shelter with permanent affordable housing. The campus will provide shelter and comprehensive services for 200 family members alongside 90 units of affordable housing, including 34 units designated for families transitioning from homelessness to stable housing.The Burien campus represents a new approach to addressing family homelessness by creating a seamless pathway from crisis to stability. Families will benefit from wraparound services including health resources, educational support, and economic empowerment programs as well as connections to schools and community networks.\"Amazon has a long history of support for Mary's Place, and we're humbled to support both Mary's Place and Mercy Housing Northwest in this project. Public-private partnerships are essential as we tackle complex challenges, including homelessness,\" said David Zapolsky, chief global affairs and legal officer at Amazon. \"Amazon is proud to support innovative solutions that make a tangible difference in our communities. This project represents a significant step forward in our collective efforts to ensure every family has a safe place to call home.\"Governor Bob Ferguson emphasized the collaborative nature of the initiative: \"Housing is too expensive in Washington, and there is not enough of it. For many Washingtonians, this campus will be a path from crisis to stability—and a place to call home.\"When the project is completed in 2027, the campus will stand as a model, here and elsewhere, for how communities can address family homelessness.\"This project represents a fundamental shift in how we approach family homelessness,\" said Dominique Alex, chief executive officer of Mary's Place. \"By collocating emergency shelter with affordable housing, we're creating a true continuum of care that allows families to rebuild their lives without the disruption of moving to unfamiliar neighborhoods. This model recognizes that stable housing is critical, and it's exactly what families tell us they need most. We are deeply grateful for the tremendous support from so many partners, funders, and advocates who have helped us reach this milestone.\"", "dateModified": "2025-09-25T22:37:14Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-19T23:33:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/c12ebc9/2147483647/strip/false/crop/3148x1771+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Ff1%2F98%2Fe73bf2d94f2092b5046d85725923%2Fimage-from-groundbreaking.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon housing fund supports groundbreaking of Mary's Place and Mercy Housing Northwest family shelter and af", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazon-housing-fund-supports-groundbreaking-of-marys-place-and-mercy-housing-northwest-family-shelter-and-affordable-housing-in-burien"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "When Amazon announced our housing commitments in 2021, we set ambitious goals to address the affordable housing crisis in our hometown communities. Today, thanks to collaboration with community organizations, public officials, and mission-aligned developers, we've reached a significant milestone: more than 10,000 affordable homes created or preserved across the Puget Sound region. What makes these investments truly special is our commitment to ensuring these properties remain affordable for 99 years—securing housing stability for generations to come.This achievement represents over $900 million in housing investments in the Puget Sound, benefitting 22,000 residents. Behind these numbers are families with access to stable, quality housing near jobs, schools, and transportation.\"When I think about these affordable homes, I see families finding stability and new opportunities,\" said Alice Shobe, director of community impact at Amazon. \"I'm grateful to celebrate this milestone with our amazing housing partners, passionate community advocates, and the local officials who've championed these efforts. These are solutions that will make a difference for generations to come.\"These 10,000 homes represent meaningful collaborations across the Puget Sound region. From our $100 million commitment to transit-oriented housing in Bellevue, to partnering with BRIDGE Housing to preserve 200 affordable apartments in Kirkland, each project responds to unique local needs. The Four Amigos Beloved Community in Seattle's Columbia City neighborhood offers multi-generational families spacious homes near light rail, with the newly-opened Connection Angle Lake with Mercy Housing Northwest providing 130 energy-efficient homes.\"Amazon's partnership has been transformative for affordable housing development in the Puget Sound region. Their approach combines critical financial resources with a genuine commitment to long-term community stability through 99-year affordability requirements,\" said Joe Thompson, president, Mercy Housing Northwest. \"At Connection Angle Lake, this partnership allowed us to create not just housing, but a community where residents can thrive near transit, jobs, and services.\"Amazon is energized by what these partnerships have accomplished and are actively seeking additional opportunities to collaborate. Amazon's housing fund is currently accepting applications for Puget Sound projects through September 30.", "dateModified": "2025-09-18T20:33:09Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-12T16:59:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/cf4577e/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4000x2250+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F1b%2F25%2F40773e8f4d94ae93c55558e66fde%2Fconnection-angle-lake-2.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Celebrating 10,000 affordable homes: Partnerships driving housing impact across Puget Sound", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=celebrating-10-000-affordable-homes-partnerships-driving-housing-impact-across-puget-sound"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Prime Video, the Seattle Seahawks, and the Smithsonian Institution brought their \"TNF for Good\" literacy initiative to Boys & Girls Clubs of King County's Ballard location.More than 125 kids were helped by Amazon volunteers to choose from a \"lawn library\" of over 1,300 books that were on display at the event. Those who attended designed bookmarks, engaged with Seattle Seahawks Legend Ben Obomanu, and took photos with Seahawks mascot Blitz.The Seattle event represents one stop in the nationwide Thursday Night Football collaboration between Prime Video, Boys & Girls Clubs of America, and the Smithsonian that aims to donate 1 million books to youth across all NFL markets in 2025. The effort reflects the partners' shared commitment to promoting literacy and fostering a love of reading among young people.\"Having a partner like the Smithsonian curate a collection of books rich in diverse stories and perspectives reflects the vibrancy of our 32 Clubs. At Boys & Girls Clubs, we use these stories to spark a love of reading that fuels imagination, and when a young person's imagination is ignited, it opens the door to a thriving, full life,\" Boys & Girls Clubs of King County CEO Lori Bae said. \"We're especially grateful to the Seattle Seahawks, who go beyond the game by sending their support with legends, dancers, and Blitz to inspire and encourage our youth. And to Amazon, who consistently shows up to ensure every young person has access to the tools, experiences, and support they need to thrive.\"\"There's something truly magical about watching a child's face light up when they find a book that speaks to them,\" said Kevin Burkhart, board member of Boys & Girls Clubs of King County and director of Echo Product & Marketing at Amazon. \"Being here today reminds me why this work matters so much. These moments create lasting impact that will extend far beyond today.\"", "dateModified": "2025-09-18T20:29:14Z", "datePublished": "2025-08-04T19:11:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/d84011e/2147483647/strip/false/crop/6000x3375+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F40%2F00%2F7b0857714bcebd5d7ac98a2a77a1%2Fbooks.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Prime Video, Seattle Seahawks, and Smithsonian team up to spark a love of reading in Seattle youth", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=prime-video-seattle-seahawks-and-smithsonian-team-up-to-spark-a-love-of-reading-in-seattle-youth"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "In celebration of the new school year, Amazon and Ascend Hospitality Group (AHG) teamed up for the fifth year of \"Ascend Has Your Back,\" providing backpacks to 800 local students in need. Students enjoyed an afternoon at a \"Summer of Soccer\" party at Amazon Fresh in Bellevue, where they received backpacks for school, personalized their packs with school supplies and fun keychains, played soccer-influenced games, met members of the Eastside's new pre-professional soccer club Midlakes United, and enjoyed lunch courtesy of Amazon. Amazon's Chief Global Affairs and Legal Officer David Zapolsky joined Bellevue Mayor Lynne Robinson, Bellevue Deputy Mayor Mo Malakoutian, and AHG Co-Founder and CEO Elaina Morris at the event. Five nonprofit partners—Boys & Girls Club of Bellevue, Rainier Athletes, Bellevue Family YMCA, Bellevue Lifespring, and 4 Tomorrow—also participated alongside dozens of Amazon volunteers to help students prepare for the school year. Amazon Fresh provided hundreds of healthy snacks for students to take home. \"Education is a huge part of what we care about, and we want to do what we can to ensure access to education for all students in our communities,\" said David Zapolsky, Chief Global Affairs and Legal Officer at Amazon. \"In 2017, we started Amazon Future Engineer, and we've been able to reach more than 2 million students around the world, including students in Bellevue, with everything from elementary school exposure to STEM to post-secondary scholarships. Volunteers gathered to harvest crops in support of food security efforts across the Puget Sound and in return, receive a free ticket to Bumbershoot. The initiative builds on last year's success, which resulted in 127,000 pounds of food collected for local food banks. This year, the program aims to mobilize more than 2,000 community volunteers to support food banks in delivering 15,000 meals. As a thank you, each volunteer will receive a free two-day festival ticket. It's a powerful incentive to address one of our region's most pressing challenges—one in 10 Washingtonians faces food insecurity. \"Seattle's arts and music scene is core to this city's identity and an integral part of our cultural fabric,\" said David Zapolsky, Amazon chief global affairs and legal officer. \"We're extending Bumbershoot Gives Back, powered by Amazon, mobilizing 2,000 volunteers and employees to provide 15,000 meals to those in need across 14 food banks in the Puget Sound. This initiative reflects how the arts can be a catalyst for addressing critical needs in our communities, and Amazon's commitment to supporting local traditions and the food security of our neighbors. When we combine Seattle's creative spirit with direct community action, we can transform the joy of music into meaningful impact for those who need it most.\" Since stepping in as a title sponsor in 2023 following a four-year hiatus, Amazon has helped revitalize Bumbershoot, making it more accessible through reduced ticket pricing and providing thousands of free tickets to underserved communities. The festival now welcomes approximately 40,000 attendees over Labor Day weekend, August 30-31. Volunteers fanned out across downtown Bellevue, collecting trash and debris to enhance the city’s shared spaces.“We are grateful for the opportunity to be part of the 'Keep Bellevue Beautiful' initiative,” said Beryl Tomay, VP of Amazon Transportation. “Our volunteers' efforts reflect Amazon's commitment to the communities where we live and work.”These park and neighborhood cleanups represent just a fraction of GMV's impact. From joining Backpack Brigade to pack weekend food bags for Seattle students who rely on school meals, to hosting mock interviews and resume building sessions for adults in need of work, employees worldwide volunteered alongside community organizations where support is needed most.Amazon’s community engagement extends beyond volunteer hours. We're working side-by-side with community partners to increase access to affordable housing, alleviate hunger, strengthen education, and help those impacted by natural disasters when they occur. "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "When 100 middle and high school girls from across the Seattle area arrived at the Virginia Mason Athletic Center this month, they found more than just the Seahawks' practice facility–they discovered a world where sports and technology seamlessly intersect.Tech Touchdown, a collaboration between Amazon Web Services (AWS), the Seattle Seahawks, and nonprofit Play Like a Girl, transformed the Seahawks training center in Renton into an interactive learning lab where young athletes explored how cloud technology powers modern sports experiences.\"It was an awesome and inspiring day at the Seahawks Training Facility,\" said Julia White, AWS chief marketing officer. \"Helping these talented young women see themselves in both sports and STEM was incredibly rewarding.”Participants rotated through three AWS-powered stations. At My Cause My Cleats, students used generative AI to design custom cleats similar to those NFL players wear for charitable causes, each taking home a custom design keepsake. The NFL Football Throw station tested quarterback abilities with sensor-equipped footballs that feature the same technology used to track throws by NFL players. Finally, at a Seahawks Hologram station, participants could “interact” with former Seahawks wide receiver Ben Obomanu and see themselves transformed into holograms.The day also featured appearances by current Seahawks’ players, including wide receiver Dareke Young and linebacker Boye Mafe who participated in flag football drills alongside students.\"We're going to show him how it's done!\" one "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "David Zapolsky, Chief Global Affairs & Legal Officer at Amazon, joined Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell and local housing developers Great Expectations and Urban Black, to break ground on Acer House, a 121-unit mixed-use community that will provide affordable homes and micro-retail spaces for local entrepreneurs in Seattle’s Central District.Made possible through a $12.6 million low-rate loan from Amazon's housing fund, the development represents an innovative approach to creating affordable housing without traditional funding mechanisms like tax credits or subordinate public loans. Acer House will serve households earning between 40% and 80% of Area Median Income (AMI), and feature six commercial spaces for small local businesses.\"By combining affordable housing with opportunities for local entrepreneurs, this project helps preserve the cultural fabric of the Central District while creating pathways for economic mobility,\" said David Zapolsky. \"We're honored that Amazon's housing fund could help make this vision a reality.”The project leverages the City of Seattle's Multi-Family Tax Exemption program and fulfills the City’s Mandatory Housing Affordability requirements. What makes Amazon's housing investment particularly impactful is that while many public funds specify affordability for 15 to 40 years, Amazon's housing fund ensures affordability for 99 years on most developments.Acer House is a recent example of Amazon's commitment to affordable housing. Since launching with an initial $2 billion commitment in 2021, Amazon has pledged $3.6 billion toward creating or preserving 35,000 affordable homes across the Puget Sound, National Capital, and Nashville regions. In the Puget Sound region alone, Amazon has committed $780 million to support more than 9,900 affordable homes, with $230 million dedicated specifically to Seattle.", "dateModified": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Mayor Harrell announces a combined $1 million investment by the City, The Asian American Foundation (TAAF), and Amazon to improve “blocks, businesses, and lives” in the heart of the historic neighborhood.In a shared effort to support safety, economic revitalization, and community well-being in one of Seattle’s most historic and culturally rich neighborhoods, Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell, TAAF, Amazon, and the Chinatown International District Business Improvement Area (CIDBIA) announced the Chinatown-International District (CID) Ambassadors Program, launching June 1, 2025.The CID Ambassadors Program will train neighborhood ambassadors to serve Japantown, Chinatown, and Little Saigon. These teams will work block by block to assist small businesses, welcome visitors, and foster safer, more vibrant neighborhoods.\"The Chinatown-International District is a vital part of Seattle's cultural and economic fabric and Amazon is proud to support the CID Ambassadors Program as part of our contributions to the revitalization of this important neighborhood,” said David Zapolsky, Chief Global Affairs and Legal Officer of Amazon. “Our investments in Friends of Little Sài Gòn and to revive the CID Night Market reinforce our commitment to fostering economic development, public safety, and cultural celebration.”This also marks the launch of TAAF’s new “TAAF Cities” initiative to spur historic public and private investment to strengthen cities with large Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) populations, of which Amazon is a founding partner. “TAAF Cities” bring together local community, government, business, and nonprofit leaders to develop innovative, community-rooted solutions that promote economic opportunity, safety, and cultural pride. Seattle, a major cultural and economic hub with some of the nation’s oldest and most dynamic AAPI communities, was chosen to kickstart the effort.”", "dateModified": "2025-09-16T23:46:02Z", {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Puget Sound Business Journal named Josh Hirschland, principal product manager for Amazon Community Impact, a 40 Under 40 honoree for his innovative work addressing food insecurity in the Puget Sound community.As the Amazon Community Delivery program lead, Hirschland applies his logistics expertise to create sustainable, scalable solutions that help get meals directly to the doorsteps of community members in need. Under his guidance, Amazon has offered pro bono delivery for more than 40 million meals to people experiencing food insecurity since 2020—2 million meals across the Puget Sound region.The program launched during the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic when food pantries faced unprecedented demand and many families couldn't leave their homes. Hirschland leveraged Amazon's last-mile delivery network to bridge this gap, transforming an initial pilot into a fully operational program within weeks.Today, Amazon Community Delivery partners with more than 35 organizations in 16 states and expanded to the UK last year. In the Puget Sound region, Amazon collaborates with seven different food banks, including United Way of King County, to serve more than 4,000 families.Amazon believes everyone should have access to healthy food and continues to address the rising rates of food insecurity by partnering with local organizations. Learn more about how Amazon delivers meals to families in need across Puget Sound.", "dateModified": "2025-09-16T23:37:29Z", "datePublished": "2025-05-08T13:49:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/5c4e92b/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1248x702+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F9b%2F98%2F7e138f8b4caf95941ecb4bea2215%2Fimg-9600.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon's Josh Hirschland named to Puget Sound Business Journal's 40 Under 40", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazons-josh-hirschland-named-to-puget-sound-business-journals-40-under-40"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Jared Johnson of Chinatown Preservation & Development talks with Seattleites about Beacon Pacific Village, an affordable housing community with a childcare center and healthy aging and wellness center, situated close to transit, schools, healthcare, and shopping. "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon's continued partnership with the Seattle Storm is supporting the second annual series of Community Hoops clinics—a collaborative effort to bring free, accessible basketball programming to youth across the Puget Sound region.As part of the collaboration, 45 youth participants from Mary’s Place participated in a clinic at the Storm’s Center for Basketball Performance in Seattle’s Interbay neighborhood—many of them experiencing the sport for the first time. A five-week series at the Family First Community Center in Renton welcomed 50 youth weekly from the Benson Hill neighborhood. In Tukwila, 88 students from elementary to high school levels came together for a day of skills-building and community.“The kids had a wonderful time and were so grateful for the experience,” said Zoë Bill, Youth Services Director at Mary’s Place. “Beyond enjoying basketball, they walked away feeling celebrated and a part of something special. Opportunities like this have a lasting impact.”The Seattle Storm’s youth basketball program, Jr. Storm, leads the clinics—putting together drills, offering encouragement, and sharing their love for the game. Their involvement helps create an inspiring environment where kids not only learn basketball fundamentals but also connect with role models.In 2024, Amazon and the Storm hosted eight clinics in partnership with local organizations—reaching hundreds of young athletes and their families. These clinics are just one example of the many ways Amazon invests in local communities across the Puget Sound. We remain committed to supporting neighborhoods where our employees and customers live and work through collaboration with local organizations and programs that create meaningful opportunities for future generations.", "dateModified": "2025-08-28T22:22:57Z", "datePublished": "2025-05-01T20:44:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/e63ff14/2147483647/strip/false/crop/7008x3942+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F59%2F89%2Fbd5dbff14cec8f690ec146b8c11d%2F20240925-jr-storm-community-clinic-marys-place-fs-45.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon and the Seattle Storm engage Puget Sound youth through Community Hoops clinics", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazon-and-the-seattle-storm-engage-puget-sound-youth-through-community-hoops-clinics"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon's investment will help two local food banks expand their capacity to serve community members with essential groceries and services.Amazon provided $2 million to support the growth of two food banks in the Seattle region, with grants of $1 million each to White Center Food Bank and Rainier Valley Food Bank. The donations will help both organizations expand their capacity to provide more community members experiencing food insecurity with vital groceries.White Center Food Bank will use Amazon's grant to support its move to their new, permanent facility in downtown White Center. The location provides improved accessibility through nearby public transportation routes and allows the organization to expand its services.Rainier Valley Food Bank will use its grant to support the renovation and expansion of its facility. The improvements will create a community food hub with that can meet urgent food needs with increased capacity for food storage, a commercial kitchen, and gathering spaces to build community.In addition to supporting the critical work of local food banks, Amazon is committed to addressing food insecurity through multiple initiatives across the communities where we operate. We collaborate with nonprofit organizations, leverage our logistics expertise, and engage our employees to help provide millions of meals to individuals and families in need. Since 2020, our Community Delivery program, which provides free delivery of charitable groceries, has delivered more than 40 million meals with 35 partners in 16 states.Learn more about Amazon's commitment to food security and how we’re helping fight hunger.", "dateModified": "2025-09-11T19:46:39Z", "datePublished": "2025-04-22T16:42:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/f25355f/2147483647/strip/false/crop/3648x2052+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F69%2F35%2Fbca51f104769af668a5654ad482b%2F20240624-hq1-hunger-rachel-load-stills-v1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon gives $2 million to support critical food security initiatives in Seattle region", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-seattle-bellevue-puget-sound-news?p=amazon-gives-2-million-to-support-critical-food-security-initiatives-in-seattle-region"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon is committing $100 million in the City of Bellevue to accelerate the production of affordable housing for low-to-moderate income families. "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon teams returning to offices are playing a major role in the continued revitalization of downtown Seattle. New data is showing a surge in foot traffic—benefiting local businesses and the broader downtown economy.The Downtown Seattle Association (DSA) reports that weekday worker foot traffic in South Lake Union and the Denny Triangle reached 74% of January 2019 levels, up from 62% the previous year. Across downtown, foot traffic increased 9% from January 2024, reaching 57% of pre-pandemic levels. Nearly 2 million unique visitors came downtown in January, 14% more than the previous year.Amazon’s presence has been a key driver of this uptick. Our 50,000 Seattle employees, along with those in Bellevue and Redmond, are bringing a sense of renewed vibrancy to these communities. Amazon teams are also feeling energized by the shift—excited by the innovation, collaboration, and connection that takes place with teams collaborating in-person.Many local business owners are noticing a welcome increase in weekday customers. “Coffee shops are full, lunch spots are buzzing, and there’s more activity in the area,” noted Downtown Seattle Association President and CEO Jon Scholes.You can read more about Amazon’s return to office plans as we continue to spark energy and innovation on behalf of Amazon’s customers here.", "dateModified": "2025-02-26T21:53:44Z",
