"http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This week, Amazon sites across the country are glowing green—a simple yet powerful symbol of appreciation for veterans, active-duty service members, military families, and those who support them.As part of the fifth annual Operation Green Light for Veterans, led by the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO), Amazon is illuminating more than 200 sites and buildings nationwide from November 4–11. The initiative honors the contributions of veterans and their families and shines a light on the importance of gratitude in our communities.\"Operation Green Light is an opportunity to thank veterans for both their service to our nation and their contributions to our communities,\" said NACo Executive Director Matthew Chase. \"With hundreds of counties coming together to light up everything from courthouses to bridges, we hope veterans will see and feel our gratitude for their selflessness and courage. We thank Amazon for their continued support and partnership in helping honor and uplift those who have served.\"At HQ2, this effort reflects Amazon's commitment to being a good employer and neighbor. With more than 6,500 members across the D.C., Maryland, and Virginia area, our employee-led group Warriors at Amazon embodies the spirit of service—supporting one another and giving back long after their time in uniform.\"What makes Operation Green Light powerful isn't just the color, it's the connection. Across more than 600 chapters and 20,000 members globally, Amazon's Warriors network continues to unite Amazon employees who lead with gratitude and show up with purpose. This moment is a thank-you to every veteran, family member, and ally who stands in support of the military community, today and every day,\" said David Sopczak, president of Warriors at Amazon.To our veterans, service members, and the Warriors at Amazon community—thank you for your courage, leadership, and the light you bring to Amazon every day.", "dateModified": "2025-11-06T17:53:35Z", "datePublished": "2025-11-06T17:53:35Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/174d0c8/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1320x743+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fbd%2F57%2F2d4ea5f64ab38a99848b590c52a5%2Fhq2-green-light4.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon glows green to honor veterans through Operation Green Light", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-glows-green-to-honor-veterans-through-operation-green-light"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon is dedicated to ensuring communities have reliable access to food year-round. As part of our overall commitment, we’ve donated an additional $1.5 million+ to help local food banks in the Northern Virginia and Washington D.C. metro area to meet increased demand. Throughout 2025, we’ve also used our transportation network to deliver more than 1.5 million meals to local organizations tackling food insecurity and have donated more than 2.9 million meals to help those experiencing hunger in the National Capitol Region.\"With recent demand on local food banks reaching new levels, supporting reliable access to nutrition has never been more critical,” said Bettina Stix, Amazon’s global director of community impact. “By combining these donations with Amazon’s free home delivery and bulk delivery services offered to local food banks, we’re helping our partners meet immediate needs while also strengthening the region’s food system.”The contributions will support key regional organizations addressing food insecurity, including Capital Area Food Bank, Bread for the City, Arlington Food Assistance Center, DC Central Kitchen, Martha's Table, and So Others May Eat. These funds will help provide thousands of families with nutritious meals, holiday food assistance, and year-round hunger relief services.“Amazon’s contribution to our Holiday Helpings will provide over 18,500 local families with turkeys and other trimmings for the Thanksgiving holiday,” said Ashley Domm, Chief Development Officer, Bread for the City. “We originally planned to support 15,000 families this year, but because of generosity like Amazon's, we were able to go the extra mile. We are happy that Amazon was able to again provide this for our community this year.”Amazon helps food banks reach more people using our established last-mile infrastructure and logistics expertise. On top of financial donations, our delivery network—including Delivery Service Partners, Amazon Flex, and Amazon Shipping—has delivered more than 60 million meals directly to over 200,000 families experiencing food insecurity across the United States since 2020. We also provide bulk transportation services for free, moving tens of thousands of pounds of food weekly between suppliers, food banks, and community distribution points using our fleet of vehicles. In addition, our grocery stores provide excess food donations to food banks around the region. Together, these efforts help ensure food reaches families in need regardless of transportation costs, barriers, or mobility challenges.Learn more about Amazon’s commitment to food security.", "dateModified": "2025-12-31T18:13:20Z", "datePublished": "2025-11-06T16:59:47Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/9025f1f/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1220x686+0+63/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fe9%2F22%2F1243d8e149e19d8b6b2c4b8066a1%2Faerial-view-of-met-park.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon provides an additional $1.5 million to support National Capital Region food banks", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-provides-an-additional-1-5-million-to-support-national-capital-region-food-banks"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon proudly partnered with DC Startup & Tech Week (DCSTW) and Arlington Economic Development (AED) to celebrate the special tenth anniversary of this influential conference, hosting a full day of programming at HQ2. DCSTW has been a catalyst for regional innovation, creating vital connections between investors, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders across the DMV area and beyond.On Thursday, October 23, Amazon HQ2 hosted a full day of programming focused on innovation, entrepreneurship, and the future of work. As a leading sponsor alongside AED, Amazon facilitated sessions that strengthened ties with the region’s top innovators and local organizations.“We’re thrilled to partner with DC Startup & Tech Week,” said Patrick Phillippi, Head of Community Engagement for Greater Washington at Amazon. “Hosting the region’s leading founders and innovators at Amazon’s HQ2 reflects our deep commitment to empowering local entrepreneurship, fostering collaboration, and shaping the next chapter of innovation in the DMV.”HQ2 Event highlights included:An exclusive networking session featuring 10 local startups pitching to investorsFounder workshops and investment discussionsPolicy conversations led by Arlington Economic Development Director Ryan TouhillSessions exploring AI, automation, and emerging technologiesInnovation spotlights celebrating women leaders driving change in techThroughout the day, discussions centered on artificial intelligence, automation, and the rapidly evolving technology landscape. Attendees engaged in dynamic fireside chats and policy conversations that highlighted the region’s growing startup ecosystem.This partnership exemplifies Amazon's commitment to investing in local communities—supporting founders, fostering innovation, and helping entrepreneurs transform ideas into impact.", "dateModified": "2025-11-05T23:53:54Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-31T15:30:50Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/cc18c42/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1116x628+42+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F1f%2F70%2Fccbc651f45d38376b8694beed84f%2Fadobe-express-file-3.png", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon welcomes DC Startup & Tech Week 2025 at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-welcomes-dc-startup-tech-week-2025-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon HQ2, Hispanic Heritage Month wasn’t just celebrated, it was lived. From lively evenings at Metropolitan Park to cultural showcases inside HQ2, each moment reflected the energy, connection, and creativity that define the Latino community across the DMV. Together with local partners, employees, and neighbors, Amazon spent the season amplifying voices, sharing stories, and honoring traditions that continue to shape our region.Every Thursday this fall, Metropolitan Park transformed into a vibrant gathering place for NaLa Noches, a Latin music series sponsored by Amazon and the National Landing Business Improvement District. Families, neighbors, and Amazonians came together as the park pulsed with rhythm and color, filled with boleros, salsa, and Venezuelan jazz from artists like Miramar, Alfredo Mojica, and Bio Ritmo. Guests enjoyed tacos and tres leches cake from local favorite Taqueria Xochi, along with Topo Chico Sabores, while the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington led hands-on art activities inspired by Día de los Muertos. The series captured what makes National Landing special—people from all walks of life gathering to celebrate culture and community.That same spirit carried inside HQ2, where the DMV Latinos employee-led group hosted Taste of Latin Cultura III, transforming the space into a celebration of music, flavor, and belonging. Amazonians heard from HQ2-based Latino leaders—including Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President of Public Policy, and Gonzalo de Dios, Head of Licensing & International Regulatory Affairs at Project Kuiper—alongside Michel Zajur, CEO of the Virginia Hispanic Chamber of Commerce, who spoke on representation, entrepreneurship, and culture’s role in innovation.The celebration continued through Expressions and Compass culinary workshops with Executive Chef George Gomez, where employees explored Latin flavors. The season may have ended, but the rhythm remains, in the community we support and the spaces it creates for cultures to shine.", "dateModified": "2025-10-24T16:14:43Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-24T16:08:37Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/2d7ba20/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1320x743+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fdf%2F20%2Fcfd991724205b736737d609f945a%2Fabout-amazon-promo-feature-hero-aws-rb.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Celebrating Hispanic Heritage Month at HQ2: a season of music, flavor, and cultura", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=celebrating-hispanic-heritage-month-at-hq2-a-season-of-music-flavor-and-cultura"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Just steps away from Amazon’s HQ2 campus in National Landing, the AWS Skills Center Arlington is celebrating a major milestone: three years of empowering learners, growing the region’s tech workforce, and building community.Since opening in 2022, the AWS Skills Center has become a flagship investment in workforce development for HQ2, welcoming thousands of visitors and providing free cloud computing training to learners of all backgrounds—no prior experience required. Its mission aligns closely with Amazon’s broader commitment to making HQ2 not just a place to work, but a hub for economic opportunity and community impact across the DMV region.On October 23, 2025, the AWS Skills Center will host a full day of free, public events at 1550 – G Crystal Drive in Arlington, inviting the community to celebrate this milestone together.The day begins with “Give Back Together,” a community service project assembling hygiene kits for local families in need. Later in the afternoon, a “Vision & Voices” panel will feature distinguished organizations reflecting on the center’s journey and exploring future workforce development initiatives.The celebration continues later in the day with an inspiring learner panel, networking with industry professionals, delicious food, and exciting giveaways.As Amazon continues to grow its HQ2 presence, the AWS Skills Center plays a critical role in building a pipeline of local talent and expanding access to tech skills, ensuring that the opportunities created here benefit the entire community.The event is free and open to everyone. Join us as we celebrate three years of learning, impact, and community-building at HQ2’s doorstep.Register now on the site.", "dateModified": "2025-10-20T17:08:48Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-20T17:08:48Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/bebe379/2147483647/strip/false/crop/8256x4644+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F9c%2F55%2Fd4dad4764925b5235c25d6007c4a%2Fztc-7113a.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "AWS Skills Center Arlington celebrates 3 years of building a skilled HQ2 community", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=aws-skills-center-arlington-celebrates-3-years-of-building-a-skilled-hq2-community"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon HQ2 recently welcomed more than 250 leaders from government, industry, and the cybersecurity community to the WAS17 Event Center for Uniting Women in Cyber (UWIC) 2025. As a lead sponsor, Amazon was proud to provide a platform for experts to connect—through both relationships and technology, to strengthen global cyber preparedness and resilience.Throughout the day, participants engaged in deep technical discussions and thoughtful policy conversations on how to protect nations, organizations, and communities from evolving cyber threats. This year’s conference placed a spotlight on senior executives leading cybersecurity innovation across critical sectors.Amazon's Hart Rossman, vice president of Global Security Services at AWS, spoke on a panel that discussed cybersecurity collaboration in a globally interconnected economy.“AWS revolutionized computing with the invention of the world’s first public cloud nearly two decades ago. Today, millions of users build their businesses on our secure and compliant cloud and AI services, making us an integral part of the global supply chain. I was energized to connect with other leaders at this event and discuss how AWS’s Shared Responsibility Model and innovative cybersecurity strategies are transforming cloud security at global scale,” said Rossman.Amazon is deeply committed to empowering women in technology and advancing careers in cybersecurity. Through comprehensive internal programs, targeted mentorship opportunities, and initiatives led by our Women at Amazon employee-led group, we’re fostering an environment where women can thrive in technical roles and leadership positions.Located in the heart of the DMV, HQ2 continues to serve as a hub where public and private sector leaders come together to build partnerships that strengthen communities and drive progress. By partnering with UWIC, Amazon is helping to elevate diverse voices, spark meaningful collaboration, and advance cybersecurity innovation—reflecting our ongoing mission to be a good neighbor and employer in the region.", "dateModified": "2025-10-13T20:17:41Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-13T20:17:41Z", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "This National Disability Employment Awareness Month, an Amazon operations facility in Northern Virginia near the companies HQ2 offices was honored as Employer of the Year from the Virginia Department for the Blind and Vision Impaired (DBVI), highlighting Amazon’s ongoing efforts to be the earth’s most inclusive and accessible employer for people with disabilities.During a celebration event on Wednesday, October 8, the DBVI recognized Amazon’s RMN3 operations facility in Stafford, Virginia for the accommodations the facility’s team has made to empower blind and vision impaired employees. The recognition underscores Amazon’s commitment to building a diverse and inclusive workforce across the Northern Virginia region, where HQ2 is helping drive job creation and opportunity.At the same event, four of RMN3’s visually impaired employees, Mavis Williams, Alice Bickersteth, Antonette Johnson, Nacel Johnson, were also honored as the DBVI’s Employees of the Year. They discussed the positive impact Amazon’s efforts have on their livelihoods and how their jobs have afforded them to ability to lead independent lives.Amazon is proud to be named the DBVI’s Employer of the Year and is committed to investing in its employees in Northern Virginia and providing a safe and welcoming workplace.", "dateModified": "2025-11-10T19:54:24Z", "datePublished": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Nearly three years into our partnership with Aspire, Amazon is proud to support the grand opening of Aspire's STEM Makerspace.On September 30, Amazon joined community leaders, educators, students, and families to celebrate the official opening of Aspire Afterschool Learning's Makerspace. The first no-cost community space for STEM programming in the area, the makerspace was created in response to the needs and input of the community.The new space expands STEM programming and hands-on learning opportunities, enables Aspire to support additional students in its programs, and provides a central meeting place for Aspire staff, students, and families.Amazon provided the initial funding for the project to enable Aspire to expand its facility and add the makerspace. With the new space, Aspire is incorporating science labs, coding classes, video and design projects, robotics demonstrations, structured play, and other interactive activities into its programming.Aspire served a record number of students during its 2024-2025 programs, with an average of 72% improving their math levels over the past two years. With community support, Aspire has sustained a 119% increase in enrollment over the last two years — in addition to expanding its fall STEM programming.\"Amazon is honored to support Aspire's work to ensure that all children have the support they need from family, school, and the community,\" said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement at Amazon.With the opening of the Makerspace, Aspire is taking another step to bridge the \"imagination gap\" for historically underserved 3rd-8th-grade Arlington students through exposure to STEM fields.", "dateModified": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, the Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) is bringing our good neighbor mission to life—one event, one partnership, and one young leader at a time. This year, BEN-DMV partnered with Together at Amazon for their second annual Day of Service, joining forces with Everybody Wins DC (EWDC), The Distant Relatives Project, and Cardz for Kidz to promote youth literacy and support those experiencing homelessness. The event united more than 200 HQ2 volunteers for a day of service and impact. Volunteers assembled 1,000 hygiene kits, packed more than 500 books with personal notes to share with local schools, and created more than 600 cards for hospitalized children. \"Representation through service means creating spaces where our communities can see, hear, and feel that they matter. Giving back locally is how we carry that forward, and the Tech Day of Service reflects the legacy we are building, one grounded in visibility, voice, and lasting impact,\" said Tyson J. Thompkins, director community engagement of BEN-DMV. BEN-DMV's impact goes far beyond one event. From cultural celebrations to neighborhood cleanups, the group builds programs that reflect HQ2's vision: a hub of innovation and inclusion that strengthens community. So far this year, they've hosted 59 events, partnered with more than 40 organizations and businesses, and contributed more than 3,600 volunteer hours—all while creating space for employees to lead with purpose and pride. \"We're not just redefining what employee care looks like in the workplace—we're demonstrating how that same commitment can fuel meaningful change in the world around us,\" said Cash Ashley, president of BEN-DMV. As HQ2 grows, BEN-DMV is helping define what it means to show up for neighbors—with heart, culture, and intention.", "dateModified": "2025-10-01T15:57:52Z", "datePublished": "2025-10-01T15:57:52Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/41bc6ff/2147483647/strip/false/crop/6426x3615+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F62%2Ffb%2F7d92edc04c2992af4884762e46be%2Fimg-8469-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon's Black Employee Network builds community at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazons-black-employee-network-builds-community-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Met Park came alive over the weekend as Amazon's third annual HQ2 Community Day transformed our Arlington neighborhood into a vibrant festival of connection and creativity. The event wasn't just a celebration; it was our heartfelt thank-you note to the community for welcoming Amazon to the neighborhood. Imagine the scene: Children bouncing through a giant foam pit, their laughter mixing with the beats of a live DJ, while parents watched their little ones become temporary artists at the LEGO wall and face-painting stations. The air was sweet with cotton candy and filled with thousands of bubbles dancing in the autumn breeze.The Think Big Experience brought AWS technology to life, turning complex concepts into wonder. Young minds were captivated as they controlled robots, wrote their first lines of code, and marveled at The Science Guys of Baltimore's spectacular shows. It was science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) education disguised as entertainment!Food enthusiasts found their paradise at the Eat Loco Farmers Market and Met Park food venues, where local flavors took center stage. As families lounged on picnic blankets enjoying discounted treats, the festival atmosphere was perfect for an afternoon of community bonding.Amazon Alley showcased local entrepreneurship, featuring DMV-based businesses like Scarlettwares, Mocktail Club, and Bon AppeSweet. Starting in 2026, ACHS will finally have a home after years of operating without one.The celebration began with a warm reception catered by local restaurant Peruvian Brothers, setting the stage for an afternoon filled with community spirit. Leaders reflected on the significance of this moment—not only for ACHS students, but for Arlington as a whole.ACHS currently serves more than 300 students, many of whom balance work and family responsibilities while pursuing their high school diplomas. Their future home at HQ2 will offer more than classrooms. \"This home is more than just a building,\" said Jeannette Allen, principal of Arlington Community High School. \"It is a place where our students can feel stability, belonging and pride. It is a place where we can deepen our roots, strengthen our programs and build lasting partnerships with Amazon and this amazing and thriving business and education community.\"For Amazon, the ACHS partnership builds on a long-standing investment in the region. Since announcing Arlington as the site of HQ2, the company has contributed more than $239 million to local nonprofits, schools, and organizations.\"When we chose Arlington as our home for our second headquarters, we committed to being more than just a neighbor—we wanted to be a true community partner,\" said Stephen Hartell, vp of public policy at Amazon. \"We couldn't be more thrilled to welcome Arlington Community High School students into the heart of our HQ2 campus. This partnership represents exactly what we envisioned with HQ2—creating meaningful connections that strengthen our community.\"The unveiling of ACHS's new home is more than a building announcement—it's the next chapter in a story about resilience, opportunity, and the power of community. Together, Amazon and Arlington are proving that strong schools build strong communities.", "dateModified": "2025-09-17T18:56:38Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-16T22:07:10Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/a9312d0/2147483647/strip/false/crop/8178x4600+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F84%2F2b%2Fa4384a984282afc285a592b54f1f%2Fzsc-9693a.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Arlington Community High School breaks ground at HQ2's Metropolitan Park", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=arlington-community-high-school-breaks-ground-at-hq2s-metropolitan-park"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "On Monday, August 25, 2025, in Arlington, Amazon HQ2 brought neighbors together for one of the sweetest traditions of the summer—Banana Split Day at Metropolitan Park.The community gathered around Amazon's iconic banana stand to enjoy free banana splits, crafted with local favorite Toby's Homemade Ice Cream, complete with all the toppings to make each scoop a masterpiece. Live music from talented local performers filled the park, and the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA) hosted creative, family-friendly activities that kept the afternoon lively and colorful.Banana Split Day is more than a summertime treat, it's part of Amazon HQ2's commitment to being a good neighbor, fostering community connection, and supporting local businesses and cultural organizations in National Landing. Partnerships with small businesses like Toby's and nonprofits like MoCA reflect our dedication to creating moments that bring people together while contributing to the local economy and arts scene.The event continued a tradition that began at Amazon's Seattle headquarters and has now become an HQ2 favorite. Judging by the smiles, the laughter, and the empty banana peels, it's safe to say this year's celebration was a hit. Toby's gave out 1,229 scoops to employees and community members, with over 300 served within the first hour!Thank you to our partners, neighbors, and everyone who joined us to make Banana Split Day 2025 a celebration to remember. We look forward to welcoming you back to Metropolitan Park for more events that connect, inspire, and celebrate our Arlington community.", "dateModified": "2025-09-14T02:01:59Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-13T14:01:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/26b6f47/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4032x2268+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F24%2Fa2%2F3d1f7da348beb0aa7f5988a0a195%2Fimg-7992.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon HQ2 serves up sweet summer fun with Banana Split Day", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-hq2-serves-up-sweet-summer-fun-with-banana-split-day"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Last month, Amazon HQ2 joined WJLA-TV and The Salvation Army for the annual Stuff the Bus back-to-school drive—united by one goal: helping local students start the school year ready to succeed.The event was a great success. More than 2,100 items were collected, with many community members also making online donations through 7News' Amazon Wish List. \"We've been here three years now since we opened our headquarters. We have really made a commitment to be a good neighbor and a good partner to this region,\" said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement.Amazon's momentum in Northern Virginia continues to grow. With more than 8,300 employees now based at HQ2, leaders underscored the company's dedication to investing in the community. \"It's important for us to be involved here. We've got 8,300 employees at this beautiful facility, and we've been part of the community from day one,\" said Stephen Hartell, vice president of U.S. public policy.Partnering with The Salvation Army reflects Amazon's commitment to long-term impact. Stuff the Bus goes beyond backpacks and school supplies—it's an investment in children's education, their confidence, and the future of the region.We are grateful to everyone who donated, volunteered, and spread the word. Your generosity shows how Amazon HQ2's commitment to strengthening community comes alive through meaningful action. Together, we're ensuring that students and families start the school year with the tools they need to thrive.", "dateModified": "2025-09-08T18:16:58Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-08T16:53:37Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/89c1f01/2147483647/strip/false/crop/768x432+1+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F42%2F42%2Fe8696ed1444b8f5c03047022c244%2Fimage002.png", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon employees 'Stuff the Bus' with Salvation Army to support students", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-employees-stuff-the-bus-with-salvation-army-to-support-students"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "The National Air and Space Museum set the stage for an evening where sports, science, and technology intersected to create a unique learning experience for local students. As part of Amazon's Thursday Night Football for Good initiative, the company joined forces with the Washington Commanders, NFL, and the Smithsonian to host an innovative STEM education event for Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington members. The event, part of the TNF for Good initiative, showed how sports can be a powerful catalyst for educational engagement. Local students from the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington received books and STEM kits to continue their educational journey beyond the museum walls. The event is part of a broader initiative to distribute 1 million books and educational resources to children across all 30 NFL markets this year. \"Through Amazon's TNF for Good initiative, we're not just distributing books—we're distributing dreams, possibilities, to empower future leaders,\" said Brian Kenner, director of community engagement, Amazon. The joint effort represents a shared commitment to education and opportunity in the National Capital Region, where Amazon's HQ2 has become an active community partner. By bringing together the Smithsonian's educational resources, the NFL's Inspire Change program, and the Boys & Girls Clubs' community connections, we're creating opportunities for local youth to engage with STEM in new ways. This powerful partnership between cultural institutions, sports organizations, corporate entities, and community groups demonstrates what's possible when diverse organizations unite behind a common goal. The evening stood as testament to the impact that collaborative community engagement can have in inspiring the next generation of explorers, inventors, and innovators.", "dateModified": "2025-09-02T16:43:06Z", "datePublished": "2025-09-01T15:38:50Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/1e8dee8/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2999x1687+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F32%2F1c%2F0ef4c1d3428393fe26d2ba451c0b%2Fimg-3722.jpeg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon teams up with NFL, Washington Commanders, and Smithsonian for STEM education initiative", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-teams-up-with-nfl-washington-commanders-and-smithsonian-for-stem-education-initiative"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "In 2024 a small idea took root: How can Amazon open our doors to inspire the next generation of builders? That vision came to life when iD Tech, in partnership with ProServe, AWS Security and the Inclusive eXperiences and Technology team, launched the inaugural HQ2 Summer Camp, welcoming Amazon employees and local youth into a world of hands-on STEM learning.The two-week program offered a timely solution for Amazon builders balancing work and family schedules during the summer. HQ2 transformed into a buzzing hub of creativity where students aged 7–17 explored game design, artificial intelligence, Python programming, and robotics. Campers also toured our HQ2 offices, joined leadership for career chats, and expressed their creativity through Amazon’s “Expression Lab” projects. Mornings focused on coding, robotics development, and game design. Afternoons brought team-building games, social activities, and robotics competitions.What made the camp unique was the direct access to Amazon tech leaders who shared real-world insights, showing how harnessing curiosity can inspire innovation with world changing impact. By the end, campers had built functional games, AI models, and robotics prototypes, leaving with project files, learning transcripts, new skills, confidence, and an ignited passion for technology.For Amazon employees, the camp was a way to share their workplace with their families. “My child loved every minute, and I appreciated being able to share part of my day with them,” one parent said. A camper called HQ2 “the best office in the whole world.”The program advances Amazon’s mission to foster innovation, strengthen local partnerships, and develop future talent. Through this initiative, Amazon continues its commitment to support employees' families, enrich our community, and inspire the next generation of innovators.", "dateModified": "2025-08-25T22:42:08Z", "datePublished": "2025-08-25T22:38:17Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/cfb01a2/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4608x2592+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F50%2Fea%2F53da86324d0fbbd83fea5d497c08%2Fdsc08707-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Opening doors to the next generation of builders at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=opening-doors-to-the-next-generation-of-builders-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon's HQ2, culture isn't just curated, it's created by our incredible employees who bring it to life every day. Recently, that spirit hit a high note as HQBlue, an eight-member jazz band made up entirely of Amazon HQ2 employees, delivered a soulful lunchtime performance at Met Park featuring classics like \"Fly Me to the Moon\" and \"Autumn Leaves.\" What began as casual jam sessions among coworkers quickly grew into something more. The group, made up of engineers and legal professionals, rehearsed during lunch breaks and after hours, building harmony across teams and titles. Their debut drew a crowd of more than 100 Amazon employees, turning an ordinary afternoon into a celebration of creativity, connection, and soul.Moments like this showcase what makes HQ2 more than just an office. It's a community where people are encouraged to bring their full selves to work, including their hidden talents. Whether launching a product or playing jazz, employees are shaping a workplace where multidimensional talent is welcomed and celebrated.As Amazon deepens its roots in Arlington, Virginia, it remains committed to building a vibrant environment that fosters collaboration, joy, and spontaneous connection. HQBlue is just one example of how return-to-office at HQ2 isn't about going back, it's about moving forward together.At HQ2, employees' passions don't have to stay after hours. They can take the stage.", "dateModified": "2025-08-04T18:58:00Z", "datePublished": "2025-08-04T18:58:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/eeb7027/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2400x1350+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F27%2F23%2F106141be415eb8f9df69704fe707%2F546a8278.JPEG", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon's employee jazz ensemble hits all the right notes at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazons-employee-jazz-ensemble-hits-all-the-right-notes-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon, we believe being a good neighbor means showing up for the communities where we live and work. This commitment comes to life each year during our Global Month of Volunteering—a company-wide effort to support the people and places that surround us.This May, more than 2,200 employees in HQ2 joined forces to support more than 100 local nonprofits and community partners across Arlington, Northern Virginia, and Washington, D.C. From Met Park to National Landing, teams came together to plant gardens, pack essential supplies, clean local parks, and build meaningful connections with their neighbors.Whether it was new hires teaming up with long-time Amazon employees, or employees from different departments working side-by-side for the first time, one thing was clear: service creates community.These moments of impact, both big and small, left a lasting impression. Many of the organizations we worked with shared how much they appreciated the energy and commitment HQ2 volunteers brought to each project.While Global Month of Volunteering may be over, our commitment to the community continues year-round. HQ2 is home to people who care deeply about their colleagues, their neighbors, and the future of the region we all share.Thank you to everyone who gave their time and energy this month. Your efforts remind us that volunteering isn’t just a one-time event, it’s a cornerstone of what it means to be part of HQ2.", "dateModified": "2025-06-27T17:27:28Z", "datePublished": "2025-06-27T17:27:28Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/439d53f/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fb2%2Fb8%2F78aee4194bf59bea23b4dc5a947b%2Fmonth-of-volunteering.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon’s Global Month of Volunteering at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazons-global-month-of-volunteering-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon's HQ2, being a good neighbor means showing up with humility, consistency, and purpose. One powerful example of this commitment is Warriors@Amazon, an employee-led group made up of veterans, military spouses, and allies who are using their shared dedication to service to strengthen ties with the local community.Whether through volunteerism, education, or support for those in need, Warriors@Amazon is helping to shape Amazon's presence in the National Capital Region in a way that honors the past while investing in the future.The National Memorial Day parade celebrationThis May, Warriors@Amazon proudly participated in the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., as part of Amazon's Global Month of Volunteering. The event, one of the nation's most visible tributes to fallen service members, offered a meaningful opportunity for HQ2 employees to honor those who served and continue to serve.For many in the group, it was a deeply personal experience, one that reflected both Amazon's support of veterans and its role as a community partner in the region.Growing for good: Urban agriculture at Arlington HouseJust minutes from HQ2's offices, a quiet act of service is taking root. Each month from spring through fall, Warriors@Amazon volunteers and partners with the Capital Region Sustainability chapter to tend the kitchen garden at Arlington House, part of Arlington National Cemetery. In collaboration with the National Park Service and Plot Against Hunger, the produce harvested here is donated to local food banks, supporting neighbors across Northern Virginia.This project blends history, sustainability, and community service. Dozens of Amazon employees—many with military connections—volunteer their time to grow food that helps nourish families while preserving one of the area's most meaningful landmarks.Building community, togetherFrom participating in the Race Across America to supporting local veteran-focused initiatives, Warriors@Amazon's efforts reflect Amazon HQ2's broader vision: to be a responsible and engaged community partner.As HQ2 continues to grow, Amazon remains focused on investing in the region—not just through jobs and infrastructure, but through people, relationships, and long-term community impact.Warriors@Amazon embodies that mission. Their work is consistent, compassionate, and deeply connected to the communities Amazon serves. Through groups like Warriors@Amazon, HQ2 is building more than a physical presence, it's building trust, connection, and shared purpose. Together, we're growing stronger, one act of service at a time.", "dateModified": "2025-06-18T22:34:15Z", "datePublished": "2025-06-18T22:34:15Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/dca6406/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2976x1674+1+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F6a%2Fce%2Fee7b2195437b87bfa64bcba9a092%2Fimg-0170.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Warriors@Amazon: Service, community, and commitment at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=warriors-amazon-service-community-and-commitment-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon, we’re proud to support Virginia Tech’s Institute for Creativity, Arts, and Technology (ICAT) as they bring innovative, creative experiences to life. This year, we launched a partnership with the institute to provide opportunities for collaboration, offering our Expressions Studio space at Met Park to host workshops for employees and community members alike—showcasing how creativity and technology can thrive together.Since August 2024, we’ve hosted workshops for more than 100 employees and community members. Participants engaged in hands-on sessions designed to spark creativity and encourage fresh approaches to problem-solving. These experiences allowed them to step away from their daily routines and explore new ways of thinking—transforming food waste into art and discovering other unique forms of creative expression.Beyond the workshops, the program included a visit to the Torpedo Factory Art Center, a renowned local venue known for its emphasis on innovation and artistic collaboration. By facilitating this visit, we helped further connect employees with the local art scene—reinforcing the idea that creativity is a vital component of innovation.Amazon’s HQ2 thrives on our commitment to fostering a culture of creativity and community. While Virginia Tech leads the way, we’re proud to be a supportive partner in bringing these ideas to life—helping to build an environment where both technology and the arts can flourish.Through partnerships like this, we’re supporting initiatives that push boundaries, inspire new ideas, and enrich the broader community. It’s just one example of how Amazon HQ2 works behind the scenes to amplify creative efforts that shape a brighter, more innovative future.", "dateModified": "2025-04-25T16:47:09Z", "datePublished": "2025-04-25T16:47:09Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/b3e1b6c/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4624x2601+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F75%2F36%2F9de96d7c48d1a560e71035c0a57a%2Fpxl-20241211-220915208-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Supporting Virginia Tech’s creativity and innovation at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=supporting-virginia-techs-creativity-and-innovation-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "HQ2 is a true headquarters for Amazon and a wide array of business units and teams work out of Met Park. Our more than 8,000 employees span departments including Prime Video, Public Policy, Amazon Web Services (AWS), HR, and more. They each contribute unique perspectives that shape the company’s future.Here’s a look at a few leaders driving Amazon’s vision forward:Francessca Vasquez, Vice President, AWS Professional Services and Generative AI Innovation CenterA technology leader with over 25 years of industry experience, Vasquez currently drives AWS's global consulting and product services across 48 countries, managing P&L and customer engagement for public sector, commercial, and partner operations. She excels in guiding organizations through generative AI transformations, leveraging AWS's AI/ML capabilities to design and deploy innovative solutions from startup to enterprise scale. Vasquez has been instrumental in shaping AWS's approach to delivering world-class solutions globally since joining in 2017.Kelly Day, Vice President, Prime Video InternationalKelly Day leads Prime Video’s international business, overseeing teams across 20+ countries who are focused on making Prime Video the first-stop entertainment destination for customers worldwide. Her role expanded in 2024 to include MGM+ and MGM Scripted Television teams. Day’s strategy integrates content, marketing, and partnerships to enhance the value Prime Video delivers to over 200 million Prime members globally.Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President, Public PolicyAshleigh de la Torre leads a Public Policy team supporting Amazon’s U.S. operations, logistics, and delivery businesses, and Amazon’s sustainability efforts. She and her team engage lawmakers and regulators at the local, state and federal levels on behalf of the company and our customers. Her work includes policymaker advocacy to improve transportation safety, support continued investment in transportation infrastructure, and encourage development of innovative and sustainable new technologies and delivery solutions.Rick Freeman, Vice President, Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS)With over 20 years of experience in defense and space industries, Rick oversees Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS), which will provide secure broadband services and space network solutions for government customers worldwide. KGS is the government-focused division of Project Kuiper, an Amazon initiative to deliver fast, reliable, and secure connectivity through a constellation of satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO). As a U.S. Marine Corps veteran, he is passionate about providing reliable satellite communications for mission-critical applications.Eric Orme, Vice President, Live Sports Product and Engineering, Prime VideoEric Orme has played a pivotal role in developing Prime Video’s live sports and entertainment strategy. His team delivers high-quality live experiences for global audiences, including events like the English Premier League and Thursday Night Football. Orme focuses on personalizing the sports viewing experience while ensuring the successful operation of every live event.Christine Enemark, VP & AGC, Prime VideoOver 13 years ago, when Christine joined Amazon, there were 3 lawyers supporting a new video streaming business operating in the United States. Today, she leads a global legal team of over 85 professionals across 14 countries, supporting Prime Video’s expansion into movies, TV shows, Amazon Originals, unscripted content, and live sports. She encourages her team to advise with the end customer in mind, shaping a legal strategy that aligns with Amazon’s customer-first approach. This perspective has strengthened both the team and the business.Jay Shankar, Vice President, Global Talent AcquisitionJay Shankar combines her engineering background with a customer-centric approach to recruiting. Since joining Amazon, she has led talent acquisition for key businesses like AWS, Advertising, and Prime Video. Shankar’s focus is on leveraging technology and data analytics to streamline recruiting processes and build a robust global talent pipeline. She also prioritizes inclusivity and career development, ensuring Amazon remains a leader in attracting top talent.", "dateModified": "2025-08-18T20:42:17Z", "datePublished": "2025-02-26T22:40:24Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/31b787d/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F58%2F2c%2F543c78b14aed919cd38be719744d%2Faa-francessca-headshot-1600x900-v1-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Leadership Spotlight: Amazonians driving innovation and growth at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=leadership-spotlight-amazonians-driving-innovation-and-growth-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This Valentine’s Day, Amazon HQ2 is teaming up with local favorites like Calluna Flower Truck, The Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA), Brightly Ever After, and a variety of Met Park restaurants for a festive celebration of love and community in Arlington, Virginia. As a proud neighbor, we're excited to bring everyone together with family-friendly events that celebrate the spirit of the season.Free roses and cardmaking at MoCA: MoCA is spreading the love with special giveaways and activities! From 11 a.m. ET to 2 p.m. ET, Calluna Flower Truck will hand out 500 free roses outside MoCA to both Amazonians and community members. If you're looking for the perfect gift, beautiful bouquets will also be available for purchase. After picking up your rose, head inside MoCA for a fun Cardmaking Workshop. Their team will help you craft heartfelt notes for your loved ones.Food pop-ups at Met Park: No celebration is complete without great food! On February 14, we’ll have a variety of delicious restaurant pop-ups at Met Park. Whether you're out with a friend or a special someone, come sample tasty treats and snacks to add flavor to your day.Valentine’s Day decorations at Met Park: To set the mood, we’re giving Met Park a romantic makeover with Valentine’s-themed sidewalk details and stunning floral decorations. Thanks to Brightly Ever After, the Pergolas near the dog park will be beautifully adorned, making it the perfect spot for a stroll or a photo op. Keep an eye out for vibrant decals leading you to the “I Heart Met Park” campaign page!Spread the love! Amazon HQ2 is committed to fostering creativity, connection, and community in Arlington. By partnering with local businesses like Calluna Flower Truck and Brightly Ever After, we’re helping make Met Park a place where everyone can come together and celebrate. We can't wait to celebrate Valentine’s Day with you—see you at Met Park!", "dateModified": "2025-02-04T20:24:37Z", "datePublished": "2025-02-04T20:02:44Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/bf6f02b/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fd7%2F03%2F99035f064b1fbbdb697402393432%2Fabout-amazon-inline-flowers-in-a-garden-at-hq2.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Love is in the air: Celebrate Valentine’s Day with Amazon HQ2 at Met Park!", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=love-is-in-the-air-celebrate-valentines-day-with-amazon-hq2-at-met-park"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "January 29th marks Minority Entrepreneur Day, a time to honor the contributions of minority-owned businesses. HQ2’s retailers are transforming the culinary landscape with unique flavors and cultural pride.Peruvian Brothers: Founded by brothers Giuseppe and Mario Lanzone, Peruvian Brothers serves bold, authentic Peruvian cuisine. From their food truck beginnings to a popular HQ2 restaurant, their success is built on fresh ingredients, tradition, and community connection.“From Day 1 twelve years ago, our motto has been, “we’re more than just a food truck; it’s about creating a community where our staff knows people by name, remembers their favorite orders, and shares the best parts of our Peruvian cuisine and culture through experiences like Alpaca Happy Hours.” — Giuseppe Lanzone, Co-Founder & Chief Operating OfficerToby’s is known for ice cream with a modern twist, blending international flavors into a locally-sourced menu. Their commitment to quality and community has made it a local favorite and a symbol of perseverance.“As a minority business owner, sometimes I feel we are overlooked by the community. Amazon has been very supportive every step of the way, and takes every opportunity to include us in their events. We are looking forward to a long, fruitful partnership and prosperous 2025.” Toby Bantug, OwnerTaqueria Xochi specializes in authentic Mexican street food, from tacos to tamales. Xochi’s passion for tradition and innovation makes this spot a hub for Mexican culture and hospitality.“We’re thrilled to bring the vibrant, authentic flavors of Mexican cuisine to Amazon HQ2. Beyond serving great food and drinks, our goal is to provide a welcoming space where employees and the local community can gather, connect, and feel truly at home.” — Geraldine Mendoza, Director of Operations & PartnerMakers Union offers a fun menu perfect for any occasion, including pet-friendly patio lunches. As part of one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food companies, it thrives in the HQ2 community.“Our fun and approachable menu is perfect for your next happy hour, business dinner, or lunch with your pup on the pet-friendly patio. Makers Union is proudly owned and operated by Thompson Restaurants, one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food and facilities management companies.” — Warren Thompson, President and Chairman", "dateModified": "2025-01-30T16:45:56Z", "datePublished": "2025-01-29T19:55:32Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/8aa84c4/2147483647/strip/false/crop/580x326+1+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Ff3%2F85%2F5f5040d845b9a2a956bdc0c9041c%2Fperuvian-brothers-at-amazon-hq2-by-sylvia-sabogal-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Celebrating minority entrepreneurs at Amazon’s HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=celebrating-minority-entrepreneurs-at-amazons-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "2024 was an exciting year for us and our partners in the National Capital Region. As we celebrate the new year, we want to take a moment to reflect on our collective impact, express gratitude to our neighbors and community partners who continue to welcome us with open arms, and acknowledge the work of so many Amazon employees who are working hard to serve our customers and communities.In 2024, we welcomed three new small businesses, and we now have 14 retailers open at Met Park—most of which are women and minority-owned. Local businesses are already experiencing a 13% increase in foot traffic and 1 million additional visits to the region since HQ2 opened its doors, and we’re excited for this growth to continue as Amazon employees work from the office more frequently in 2025.We remain deeply committed to the National Capital Region, donating more than $78 million to community groups and nonprofits in 2024 alone. As a result, we were honored to be named the top corporate philanthropist for the second year in a row by the Washington Business Journal.We were also proud to receive LEED Platinum v4 certification from the U.S. Green Building Council, making Amazon HQ2 the largest newly constructed building in the world to achieve this distinction. This milestone reinforces our commitment to sustainability, while representing a meaningful step towards decarbonizing Amazon’s real estate portfolio in order to meet our Climate Pledge commitment.As we enter the new year, we’re inspired by the innovation, collaboration, and connection that we’ve already seen with our teams working in person together. With most employees working from the office each day, we look forward to continuing to support local small businesses while contributing to economic growth and the overall vibrancy of National Landing.We are incredibly proud of everything our teammates are accomplishing, as well as our ongoing commitment to our customers, employees, and the communities we call home. Here's to an exciting 2025 and beyond—Amazon will continue to strive to be a good neighbor and trusted business partner in the region.", "dateModified": "2025-01-13T16:07:22Z", "datePublished": "2025-01-13T16:04:04Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/6749e92/2147483647/strip/false/crop/8192x4608+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F7c%2F88%2Fe9acb27b484cbec2b4e05a5e8e9c%2Fw1a1351-copy-1-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "A year in review at HQ2: Continuing to deliver for our communities", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=a-year-in-review-at-hq2-continuing-to-deliver-for-our-communities"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon's Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) recently celebrated a monumental milestone in its ongoing partnership with the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington, hosting the third annual \"Great Big Kid\" event at Amazon's HQ2. This year’s celebration was the largest and most impactful to date, highlighting the continued growth and success of this powerful initiative.The “Great Big Kid” program, a six-week mentorship initiative, pairs Amazon volunteers with local youth, providing them with invaluable opportunities for personal and professional growth. This year’s event saw over 200 attendees, including mentees, mentors, families, and Amazon leadership, all gathering at HQ2 to commemorate the program’s extraordinary achievements.“What started as a simple sponsorship has grown into a dynamic, multi-faceted partnership,” said Cash Ashley, President of BEN-DMV. “We’ve gone from having just one mentor in the program to hosting field trips, providing multiple mentors, and even welcoming students to our AWS Skills Center.”The event featured an inspiring keynote from Keyon Young, Director at Amazon, who shared his perspective on the power of mentorship. Drawing from his experiences as an executive director in Albany, NY, and his current role at Amazon, Keyon emphasized how mentorship profoundly impacts both mentors and mentees. Building on this theme, Gabrielle Webster, CEO of Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington, spoke to the transformative nature of the partnership, stating, \"This collaboration with Amazon’s Black Employee Network goes beyond funding. It’s about building lasting connections and creating meaningful opportunities for our youth.\"The partnership’s impact reaches far beyond the annual event. Two Amazonians now hold board seats across different Boys & Girls Club regions, reinforcing the deepening connection between the organizations and ensuring continued support for the next generation.By the Numbers:3 years of partnership$155,000 total investment over three years7 Amazon mentors in this year’s program2 field trips to AWS Skills Center200+ attendees at the HQ2 celebration26 Boys & Girls Club sites represented12,000 youth impacted across the DMV areaThis event highlights the impact we can achieve as a community. The passion of our affinity group and the dedication of volunteers like Quanteer have driven three years of success. Our collaboration shows that by uniting for a common cause, we can create lasting change and opportunities for youth in need.", "dateModified": "2024-12-13T13:29:53Z", "datePublished": "2024-12-13T13:29:53Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/8a4c6a3/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4032x2268+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F73%2F55%2Ffa0af3254755bc2e7a443cfcd8ec%2Fimg-3271-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "BEN-DMV hosts third annual 'Great Big Kid' event, celebrating largest Amazon-Boys & Girls Club partnership", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=ben-dmv-hosts-third-annual-great-big-kid-event-celebrating-largest-amazon-boys-girls-club-partnership"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "This fall, Amazon hosted HQ2’s Community Day, a celebration commemorating HQ2’s one-year anniversary since opening its doors to employees and the community in National Landing.HQ2 Community Day commemorated Amazon’s ongoing dedication to being a good neighbor and community partner in the region. Since announcing plans to build its second headquarters in Arlington, Amazon has worked each day to fulfill its commitment to becoming one of the region's most trusted business and community partners. Last year, Amazon donated more than $78 million to over 100 organizations in the area, making it the top corporate philanthropist in the region for the second consecutive year.At Community Day, more than 1,000 neighbors of all ages gathered in Met Park to enjoy a variety of activities. This included a “Taste of Met Park” that highlighted the small businesses and restaurants that call Met Park home. Other activities included a community library, law games, a Lego wall, face painting, and more. With over 50 local vendors, Met Park's Saturday Market offered produce, hot food and handcrafted art. A photo booth with themed props and a live DJ kept the energy lively throughout the day.", "dateModified": "2024-11-22T17:55:41Z", "datePublished": "2024-11-22T17:55:41Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/1b6789d/2147483647/strip/false/crop/3872x2178+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F7a%2F90%2Fe7699d974450a887cb4678b24ab3%2F2024-09-21-amazoncommunityday2024-20240921-095641-3.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon's HQ2 Community Day: celebrating one year of partnership and fun at National Landing", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazons-hq2-community-day-celebrating-one-year-of-partnership-and-fun-at-national-landing"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "We are proud to welcome 21 high school students from Arlington County and D.C. to Amazon’s JumpStart Youth Apprenticeship Program in partnership with CityWorks DC, an affiliate of CareerWiseUSA. These students will work in a variety of technical and business operations roles under the guidance of several teams at Amazon’s HQ2, including Amazon Web Services, Prime Video, Finance, and Generative AI.“This region’s dedication to education and workforce training were a significant factor in our decision to select Arlington for HQ2,” said Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s Vice President of Worldwide Economic Development. “We recognize that work-based learning programs are an important component for future academic and career success, and to help close the opportunity gap for the underrepresented and underserved. We are thrilled to welcome these incredible student leaders to Amazon.”The two-year program for rising high school juniors and seniors is designed to be an options multiplier for students—accelerating both their future academic and career prospects. Throughout JumpStart, apprentices complete an 11-week cohort-based onboarding program followed by paid part-time work at Amazon as a complement to their high school coursework.“We know that talent is equally distributed, but opportunity is not,” said Jennifer C. Niles, President and CEO of CityWorks DC. “Creating a direct pathway into Amazon from our public high schools is the bold action we need to truly create a local talent pipeline into high-wage, high-demand careers. We have the talent and JumpStart is the opportunity.\"\"We are proud of our students and look forward to seeing the positive impact this experience will have on their future academic and career trajectories,” said Dr. Francisco Durán, Superintendent, Arlington Public Schools. “JumpStart is an extraordinary opportunity for our students to gain new skills working alongside professionals at one of the world’s leading companies. We are grateful for Amazon's commitment to education and look forward to seeing all our students will achieve.”“Meaningful, cross-sector collaborations like JumpStart bridge critical gaps between education systems and the workforce, and apprenticeships provide invaluable opportunities to shape the career paths and futures of our students,” said DC Public Schools Chancellor, Dr. Lewis D.
