Ferebee. “We know these experiences not only equip our young people with essential skills, but they also enhance their mobility and prepare them for success well beyond their time with DC Public Schools.\"We are excited to partner with Amazon and CityWorks DC to provide our students with real-world training in high-demand fields while still in high school,” said Patricia Brantley, CEO of Friendship Public Charter Schools. “Through this apprenticeship, Amazon will help our students gain valuable skills, earn a competitive wage, and build meaningful pathways to college and careers. At Friendship Public Charter School, we are committed to providing every opportunity for our students' success. Amazon is making that possible. We look forward to seeing our students thrive and make a positive impact at Amazon and beyond.\"Amazon’s JumpStart program first began in New York in 2019. Nearly 200 NYC students have participated in the program and are now pursuing college coursework and/or working in roles at Amazon, including in software development. Learn more about a recent Amazon JumpStart youth apprentice and Amazon UX Designer.", "dateModified": "2024-11-22T17:56:57Z", "datePublished": "2024-11-22T01:10:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/073f146/2147483647/strip/false/crop/4032x2268+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F5d%2F3a%2F35785e7a429c8b7206eb6e2aa907%2Fajs-hq2-day-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon launches high school Apprenticeship Program for D.C. and Virginia students in partnership with CityWor", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-launches-high-school-apprenticeship-program-for-d-c-and-virginia-students-in-partnership-with-cityworks-dc"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "At Amazon, we live our mission of making our customers’ lives better and easier every day. We also bring this innovative, inventive spirit to empower the communities we call home, using our scale and abilities to take on some of society’s most pressing challenges. Working side-by-side with community partners, we’re increasing access to affordable housing and alleviating hunger. We’re strengthening education, promoting literacy, and helping people gain skills for the future.Our most recent Community Impact Report showcases the progress we’re making and the positive impact we believe we’re having on everyday life for our National Capital Region neighbors. Highlights include:We contributed $78 million last year in support of more than 90 community partners dedicated to making the National Capital Region a better place to live. Through initiatives large and small, our partners’ work has an indelible impact—providing essential social services to those who need them most, ensuring young people have access to learning opportunities, revitalizing our parks and green spaces, and continuing beloved area traditions like the National Cherry Blossom Festival and the Arlington County Fair.Since launching the Amazon Housing Equity Fund in 2021, we have committed over $1.2 billion to help create or preserve affordable housing for more than 20,700 low- to moderate-income individuals so they can live in the communities they serve and love. This is especially important for first responders, teachers, nurses, and service industry employees who are getting priced out of expensive metro areas like ours.We’ve donated more than $2.7 million since 2021 to local organizations tackling food insecurity and hunger across the National Capital Region. Our support recently helped the Capital Area Food Bank build its new warehouse in Lorton, Virginia, enabling the distribution of 13 million meals annually to Northern Virginia residents experiencing food insecurity.We continue to invest in programs to help children in grades K-12, higher-education students, and adult learners unlock their potential. By focusing on critical digital skills, we prepare these individuals for the jobs of the future. Through Amazon Future Engineer— our childhood-to-career computer science program—we delivered 74,000 student hours of STEM education, literacy, and career exploration courses across the National Capital Region. And over 55,000 learners have accessed free training and education at the AWS Skills Center in Arlington.These are just a few of the ways that we are giving back to the region that’s become our second home. We are committed to supporting and collaborating with our neighbors and communities throughout the National Capital Region. We know that—working together—we can build an even brighter future.Learn more about what we’re doing for our employees, neighbors, and the community.Explore our Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region.", "dateModified": "2024-11-12T14:40:46Z", "datePublished": "2024-11-12T14:00:19Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/f907838/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2000x1125+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fee%2F98%2Fad239c074f6e8342fde1c04002f8%2Fhq2-2024-cover.png", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon showcases commitment to National Capital Region in latest Community Impact Report", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-showcases-commitment-to-national-capital-region-in-latest-community-impact-report"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon's HQ2 is glowing a vivid green again in observance of veterans of the armed forces. Starting on November 4 and running through November 11, 2024, this inspirational display symbolizes Amazon's ongoing support of Operation Green Light for Veterans. In partnership with the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO), this nationwide initiative aims to highlight the resources available to support veterans and their families while bringing attention to the significant challenges they face.Why Green?The color green is a universal representation of honor, rebirth, and unwavering courage. Lighting up our headquarters in this empowering color not only honors Veterans Day but also visually affirms our support and raises public awareness of our veterans' ongoing needs.A Global CommitmentAmazon is committed to this cause, even beyond the image. More than 150 of Amazon’s operations locations across the country are participating in this powerful and luminary initiative. Veterans Day, observed annually on November 11, invites everyone to reflect on the bravery and sacrifices made by veterans. Amazon is proud to play a role in this reflection and acknowledgment, ensuring that the valor and hardships of veterans remain at the forefront of our national conversation.\"As we light over 150 operations sites and buildings across the country for Operation Green Light, we are proud to continue our partnership with the National Association of Counties and encourage organizations across the country to join us in support of and celebration of our veterans,” said Charlotte LaBelle, Leader of Amazon’s Global Military Affairs and Army veteran. “At Amazon, we value the unique skills and experience that the military community brings to our company. This year we surpassed our goal of hiring 100,000 veterans and military spouses and are committed to continue supporting our military-affiliated community and to helping veterans transition into roles in the private sector.”Throughout the week, the green glow will represent appreciation and solidarity for residents of Arlington County, Virginia, and other participating communities. It is a simple but powerful reminder of our support to those who have served and protected us while in uniform.Thank YouOur special thanks goes out to our Warriors@Amazon affinity group, who have worked tirelessly to ensure that our military community is acknowledged. “With more than 600 chapters and 20,000 members globally, we rely on our chapter leaders to recognize this community and develop resources for transitioning veterans. We appreciate everyone who continues to stand by those who have served and are currently serving,” says Michelle Marchiano, President of Warriors@Amazon.", "dateModified": "2025-01-13T15:58:44Z", "datePublished": "2024-11-11T16:01:19Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/68cdbb9/2147483647/strip/false/crop/5667x3188+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F99%2F98%2F22b391c8461b850a140264778212%2Fimg-1321-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Operation Green Light at HQ2: A powerful partnership to honor veterans", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=operation-green-light-at-hq2-a-powerful-partnership-to-honor-veterans"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "As part of our commitment to providing a home for Arlington Community High School (ACHS) by 2026, we are finalizing plans to welcome more than 250 ACHS students, faculty, and staff to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park in time for the 2026-2027 school year. Since we selected Arlington as the home of our second headquarters in 2018, we have worked closely with the county and School Board to address one of the community’s most pressing issues—finding a location for ACHS which has never had a permanent home.“We look forward to this bright and exciting new chapter for Arlington Community High School and our students and staff. Having a new dedicated learning space, custom-built to serve their needs in an accessible location, will make a world of difference for our students as they pursue their academic and professional goals,” said Dr. Francisco Durán, superintendent, Arlington Public Schools. “We thank Amazon for their ongoing partnership in serving our students and commitment toward advancing equity and education in Arlington.”“Arlington Community High School plays a vital role in the educational and career development of these 250 students and we’re thrilled to welcome them to our second headquarters in 2026,” said Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s vice president of worldwide economic development. “By providing a new location for ACHS, we’re able to help deliver on our commitment to the Arlington community, while helping to unlock new economic and job opportunities for our neighbors, today and in the future.”Arlington Community High School serves a diverse student body of students ages 16 and older from across Arlington County, many of whom work full- or part-time jobs to support their families as they earn their high school diplomas. In addition to dedicated learning space at HQ2, students will have access to an adjacent 2.5 acre public park, community garden, dog run, and 14 local, small businesses.Amazon’s partnership with ACHS is an important piece of the company’s broad commitment to being a good neighbor and trusted community partner. We have continued to focus our effort, time, and resources on partnering with nonprofits and community groups to address challenges like equity in education, sustainability, and affordable housing. To date, we have donated more than $239 million to local community organizations and our commitment to providing a home for ACHS will also reduce the long-term operating and capital cost for Arlington Public Schools.", "dateModified": "2024-10-11T16:03:07Z", "datePublished": "2024-10-11T16:03:07Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/3d7807c/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2000x1125+0+105/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fa8%2F46%2F77b363d54193a1ad847e3e4aef49%2Fhq2-high-school.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "HQ2 prepares to welcome Arlington Community High School to Metropolitan Park in 2026", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=hq2-prepares-to-welcome-arlington-community-high-school-to-metropolitan-park-in-2026"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon Fresh has teamed up with Whole Foods Market Foundation by donating $250,000 to fund 83 school gardens across the country, including local schools across the Washington D.C. metro region. This initiative aligns with Amazon Fresh's long-term vision of positively impacting local communities by promoting healthy eating habits and educational initiatives.Among the beneficiaries are Montessori Public School of Arlington, Dr. Charles R. Drew Elementary, Oakridge Elementary, and Gunston Middle School in Arlington, as well as Herndon High in Herndon, Virginia. Hillcrest Heights ES Planet Protectors in Temple Hill, Capital City Lower Public Charter School in Washington, Takoma Park Elementary in Takoma Park, East Silver Spring Elementary School, and New Hampshire Estates Elementary School in Silver Spring will also receive Whole Kids Garden Grants. These grants will enable the schools to establish or enhance their edible gardens and engage students in hands-on learning about growing and enjoying fresh produce.\"We're thrilled to support these local schools and their efforts to cultivate a love for nutritious foods among students,\" said Jim Gillespie, regional manager for Amazon Fresh in the HQ2 region. \"By providing access to edible gardens, we aim to inspire a new generation of kids to develop an appreciation for where their food comes from.\"\"When kids have a hand in growing fruits and vegetables, they are more likely to try them, enjoy them, and inspire other kids to do the same. Additionally, kids who grow up enjoying fruits and vegetables are more likely to continue eating them through adulthood,” said Carol Medeiros, executive director of Whole Foods Market Foundation. “We’re so grateful to Amazon Fresh whose generous donation in the Whole Kids Garden Grant program this year will allow more students to get a jumpstart on a lifetime of enjoying healthy food.\"Through this collaboration, Amazon Fresh reinforces its commitment to investing in the well-being of local communities in the HQ2 region. By nurturing a love for fresh produce and sustainable practices from an early age, the company hopes to inspire positive change and contribute to a healthier, more sustainable future for all.", "dateModified": "2024-08-27T16:25:19Z", "datePublished": "2024-08-27T12:00:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/c96f24f/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2560x1440+0+133/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F21%2Faf%2F4e2218884a9da8a9b308f58171f8%2Fwhole-kids-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon Fresh cultivates healthy eating habits and educational opportunities in the Washington D.C. metro regi", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-fresh-cultivates-healthy-eating-habits-and-educational-opportunities-in-the-washington-d-c-metro-region"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "When Amazon chose Arlington as the home of HQ2, we made a commitment to be a trusted business and community partner in the region. The work to become a part of the fabric of this community reflects that commitment daily. We partner with many community leaders and organizations to ensure we’re making the most impactful contributions—our Housing Equity Fund (HEF) is a powerful example of how we can address community needs through innovative partnerships. In an effort to bring together and celebrate our community and public-sector partners, Amazon recently hosted an Affordable Housing Summit at HQ2's Amazon Visitor Landing. The summit gathered policymakers, affordable housing partners, and community leaders for a discussion about housing challenges, and how we can work together to identify and implement solutions. The event came on the heels of Amazon’s announcement that the company’s HEF would extend its commitment to include an additional $1.4 billion to create and preserve an additional 14,000 homes in our hometown communities, including Arlington.Since the HEF launched in 2021, positive impacts in the HQ2 region have been significant. To date, we have:Committed $1.1 billion to help create and preserve more than 9,400 affordable homes across the National Capital regionAmazon’s funding has helped increase affordable housing by 23% in Arlington, VA58% of Amazon-funded affordable homes across the region will serve households earning less than 60% of the area median income (AMI)More than 80 community leaders and affordable housing advocates attended the Affordable Housing Summit, which included a fireside chat with Senator Mark Warner (VA) and a panel discussion with Amazon Housing Equity Fund’s Managing Principal Senthil Sankaran, Arlington Partnership for Affordable Housing President Carmen Romero, and LEO Impact Capital President Brian Allan “AJ” Jackson. Participants focused on the importance of public-private partnerships to make a meaningful difference for thousands of families across Virginia. Both discussions were moderated by WTOP’s Capitol Hill Correspondent, Mitchell Miller.", "dateModified": "2024-08-27T16:28:39Z", "datePublished": "2024-08-26T23:00:00Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/4f8c923/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F5c%2Fc7%2F6efed58f4b7c9912133f85d7052b%2Fttas9006-1.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Highlights from Amazon’s Affordable Housing Summit at HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=highlights-from-amazons-affordable-housing-summit-at-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Met Park is home to many things—over a dozen women- and minority-owned small businesses, works of art that honor the National Capital Region, world-leading sustainable buildings, and more. Now it’s home to butterflies, too.Met Park’s Butterfly Garden opened on June 20 to a crowd of Amazon employees and their families, children from the Celebree School of National Landing, and members of the community, who all showed up to see these little natural wonders. The Butterfly Garden is a deeply precious community attraction that sits at the crossing point of two of Amazon’s priorities: being a leader in sustainability and being a good neighbor to the Arlington community.“Our 2.5-acre public park is home to a diverse range of native plants that both beautify the surrounding area and nourish the soil in which they are planted,” said Lead Horticulturist Dillan Clark. “Once we realized that our park grounds already included many of the attributes and native plants that are needed to foster a home for butterflies, we partnered with the North American Butterfly Association to explore an expansion of Amazon’s horticultural attractions and bring the butterfly garden to life.”Met Park was planned with native and adaptive plantings that are conducive to the Arlington climate—the soil is engineered to retain water and promote root growth while smart and low-flow irrigation systems are optimized to reduce water consumption by providing only what the native plants need. The garden currently includes ten Painted Lady butterflies that are currently in their chrysalises metamorphosing from caterpillar into butterfly—but they’ll be spreading their wings soon.Amazon’s second headquarters was built with sustainability at its core, including two acres (or nearly 100,000 square feet) of green roofs with native plantings where visitors and employees alike can find respite and connect to nature. The Amazon Horticulture team leads a program in partnership with the local Love and Carrots organization, grows fresh produce that gets delivered to the local nonprofit Kitchen of Purpose, with 800 pounds of produce contributing to over 600 meals per week.Thank you to the horticulturists, volunteers, and employees, who have been pivotal in the launch of Met Park’s Butterfly Garden.", "dateModified": "2024-07-05T16:58:32Z", "datePublished": "2024-07-05T16:58:32Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/3da02f0/2147483647/strip/false/crop/6000x3375+0+313/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fc0%2F29%2F6240bae94679a10d1654073b8c8e%2Fiwp2473-061.JPG", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Explore the Met Park Butterfly Garden at Amazon’s HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=explore-the-met-park-butterfly-garden-at-amazons-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "We’re now extending our commitment to the Amazon Housing Equity Fund with an additional $1.4 billion in funding to create and preserve 14,000 more homes in three of its hometown communities—Washington State's Puget Sound region; the Arlington, Virginia/National Capital region; and Nashville, Tennessee. This brings the total to more than $3.6 billion to fund more than 35,000 affordable homes across these three regions. The extension of the Housing Equity Fund underscores our commitment to affordable housing and to helping thousands of families live closer to where they work or near transportation hubs, removing a major barrier to success. To date, Amazon's Housing Equity Fund has helped create and preserve 9,500+ homes across the National Capital region and increased Arlington, VA’s affordable housing stock by 23%.Learn more about this extension of the Housing Equity Fund and how the Amazon Housing Equity Fund is helping create affordable housing across communities that Amazon calls home.", "dateModified": "2024-06-11T21:38:40Z", "datePublished": "2024-06-11T21:38:40Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/11a0487/2147483647/strip/false/crop/2000x1125+0+1/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fec%2F80%2F196f584d46068b61c21857bc2eb9%2Fweb-hero-20240227-hq1-hef-ulms-michelle-coffeeshop-stills-v1-6.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Amazon adds $1.4 billion to the Housing Equity Fund to create and preserve more affordable homes in the Natio", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=amazon-adds-1-4-billion-to-the-housing-equity-fund"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon’s Metropolitan Park at HQ2 was recently recognized as Urban Land Institute (ULI) Washington’s Excellence in Sustainability recipient during their 2024 awards for excellence. The award recognized HQ2 as a project that made outstanding contributions to development within the D.C. region, and one that exemplifies innovation and creativity that inspires vibrant communities.HQ2 was built with sustainability at its core, and we are proud that Met Park’s towers are the largest buildings in the world to be LEED Platinum v4 certified by the US Green Building Council. Met Park is all electric and powered by 100% renewable energy—including each of the 13 retailers on our ground floor. Sustainability-focused initiatives are prevalent throughout Met Park, including lower-carbon concrete, mass timber, advanced ways to reuse water, and even bird-friendly glass to protect wildlife on their important migratory path.Amazon congratulates fellow ULI award recipients who are working to collectively drive transformative change across the region.Learn more about HQ2’s sustainability-focused construction and design.", "dateModified": "2024-05-22T13:11:10Z", "datePublished": "2024-05-22T13:11:10Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/a33ef7e/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Faf%2Fe7%2Ff4930d3246cc9cec3ff32cc636de%2Fhq2-uli-sustainability.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "HQ2 receives Urban Land Institute Washington’s top recognition for Excellence in Sustainability", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=hq2-receives-ULI-sustainability-award"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Amazon’s vision for its HQ2 has been to bring together employees, local residents, and visitors to enjoy a vibrant, 18-hour neighborhood. We are proud to offer more than 50,000 square feet of retail space for 13 small businesses at HQ2’s Metropolitan Park, many of which are minority and women-owned.We were honored to welcome the U.S. Small Business Administration’s Administrator Isabel Guzman to HQ2 to meet several of the business owners and employees who are part of our retail community. Administrator Guzman serves in President Biden’s Cabinet as the leading advocate for America’s 33 million small businesses and innovative startups. Watch the recap of Administrator Guzman’s visit.", "dateModified": "2024-05-13T20:13:56Z", "datePublished": "2024-05-13T20:09:41Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/9025f1f/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1220x686+0+63/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fe9%2F22%2F1243d8e149e19d8b6b2c4b8066a1%2Faerial-view-of-met-park.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Isabel Guzman, Administrator of the U.S. Small Business Administration, visits Amazon's HQ2 retailers", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=isabel-guzman-administrator-of-the-u-s-small-business-administration-visits-amazons-hq2-retailers"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "The Innovation Studio + Store showcases contemporary art and adds to a growing list of vibrant local retailers at Metropolitan Park.Amazon is proud to welcome the Museum of Contemporary Art (MoCA) Arlington to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park. The new Innovation Studio + Store is a place for Arlington residents and visitors to learn more about contemporary art, interact with professional artists at work, participate in free public art and artist programs, explore their creativity in private classes, and shop various creative and inspiring products from the museum store.“Community is at the heart of everything we do here at Amazon. We are honored that the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington is joining HQ2 to enrich the Arlington community by connecting residents and visitors to exhibitions, educational programming, and artist residencies,” said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement at Amazon. “This is a great new chapter for all of us who live, work, and play in National Landing.”“We are thrilled to open our new Innovation Studio + Store at Amazon HQ2,” said Catherine Anchin, MoCA Arlington’s executive director. “We know that contemporary art can feel intimidating to some. This space was intentionally designed to feel warm and inviting, with the hope that people will be inspired to join us for a class, participate in a workshop, or simply support local artists.”Since coming to National Landing, Amazon has prioritized providing a home for small, local businesses, with a focus on those which are women- and minority-owned. See a full list of local retailers at HQ2’s Met Park.", "dateModified": "2024-03-11T16:33:35Z", "datePublished": "2024-03-11T16:27:58Z", "image": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/dims4/default/1ee3111/2147483647/strip/false/crop/1600x900+0+0/resize/1200x675!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Famazon-blogs-brightspot.s3.amazonaws.com%2F96%2F71%2Fe368ed7142e0886f9cd02ccfac54%2Fmoca-hq2.jpg", "mainEntityOfPage": {"@type": "WebPage", "@id": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news"}, "author": [{"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "Person", "image": {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/47/6d/7fe296e642859c7ba2a629eda3ba/amazon-logo-hero-images-2024.jpg"}, "name": "Amazon Staff"}], "publisher": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "US About Amazon", "logo": {"@type": "ImageObject", "url": "https://assets.aboutamazon.com/bd/8f/88e7b0314cc2bd05b9de3155a0fe/amazon-news.svg"}, "parentOrganization": {"@type": "Organization", "name": "Amazon", "url": "https://www.amazon.com"}}, "headline": "Museum of Contemporary Art (MoCA) Arlington studio and store now open at Amazon HQ2", "url": "https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/community/amazon-arlington-virginia-news?p=museum-of-contemporary-art-moca-arlington-studio-and-store-now-open-at-amazon-hq2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "BlogPosting", "articleBody": "Nearly one year after opening the doors to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park, Amazon recaps its community engagement activities and investments in the region.We are proud to work with nonprofits, schools, and community leaders across the National Capital region to support equity in education, affordable housing development, and regional, inclusive economic growth.We recently released Amazon’s updated Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region, where we shared details about how we’re delivering for our customers, employees, and the communities where we operate. Success and scale bring broad responsibility and as we continue to grow, we are committed to using our resources to help address important challenges that our hometown communities face.Here are a few highlights of our community engagement efforts and positive impact over the past year: We worked with more than 100 nonprofits in and around Arlington, Virginia, which is home to Amazon’s second headquarters (HQ2). Our support went to organizations like Bridges to Independence, which is working to break intergenerational cycles of poverty; the English Empowerment Center, which serves nearly 1,500 beginning-level English language and literacy adult learners; and Aspire, which helps historically underserved young people fulfill their potential through afterschool and summer learning programs that support and connect families, schools, and communities.Amazon was named the top philanthropic company in the Washington, D.C., metro area by the Washington Business Journal. Since 2018, Amazon has invested more than $161 million in local nonprofits, community groups, organizations, businesses, and public schools.Amazon launched a new housing pilot initiative that will help nearly 1,000 individuals and families from underserved communities become homeowners in Virginia and Washington, D.C., as well as Washington state and Tennessee. This is part of a broader set of initiatives from the Amazon Housing Equity Fund. Since 2021, Amazon has committed over $1 billion in loans and grants to create or preserve more than 7,500 affordable housing units in and around the National Capital Region.Amazon continued to advance its commitment to equity in education through several partnerships and funding efforts, including Amazon Future Engineer, a comprehensive childhood-to-career program aimed at increasing access to computer science education for students from underserved and underrepresented communities. Amazon helped students broaden their horizons through education via:$1.8 million in scholarships awarded to 45 Capital Region students by the Amazon Future Engineer program241 schools and 23,470 students across the Capital Region received STEM education and career exploration courses13,000+ students have access to three AWS Think Big Spaces in VirginiaAmazon has continued to support and integrate local businesses into its Arlington headquarters, representing vibrant local favorites, women- and minority-owned retailers, and others. For example, visitors can get their bike serviced at Conte’s Bike Shop, drop off furry friends at doggy daycare at District Dogs, get a workout in at HUSTLE, grab a bite to eat at Peruvian Brothers, and so much more.Amazon will continue to strive to be a good neighbor and trusted business partner across the region.Learn more about what we’re doing for our employees, neighbors, and the community.Read more in our Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region.", "dateModified": "2024-02-02T20:46:13Z", "datePublished": "2024-02-02T16:30:25Z", "image": "description": "Learn how we invest in the community, help power the local economy, and support our employees in the National Capital Region.", "headline": "Amazon in the community: Service, community, and commitment at HQ2"}, {"@context": "http://schema.org", "@type": "NewsArticle", "articleBody": "From 20 riders to 250: How Amazon’s cycling community showed up for the Armed Forces Cycling Classic When Logan Cunningham completed her first Olympic-distance triathlon race at 13 years old, she never imagined she’d one day lead a 250-person corporate team through the streets of Arlington. But that’s exactly what happened this year at the Amazon Armed Forces Cycling Classic, the Capital region’s premier cycling event and one of the largest races in the D.C. area.Amazon has served as the title sponsor since 2025. This year marked a milestone: a new partnership with Amaury Sport Organisation (A.S.O.), the company behind the Tour de France, elevating the event from a regional tradition to one with international recognition.The weekend kicked off Saturday with the Challenge Ride, open to cycling enthusiasts of all ages and abilities who participated on behalf of a corporate team, an armed forces branch, or as individuals. Sunday brought the beloved Clarendon Cup, the D.C. region’s premier professional and amateur race since 1998.Amazon boasts the largest corporate team in the event—a community that has grown more than tenfold under Logan Cunningham’s leadership.“I’m so glad I get to bring my passion for cycling to Amazon and build a cycling community among my fellow Amazonians,” said Cunningham. “It has been amazing seeing our corporate team grow from 20 riders when we first started to the more than 250 that participated this year.”The event reflects Amazon’s broader commitment to the communities surrounding HQ2, supporting military service members, bringing employees together outside the workplace, and investing in the vibrancy of the Capital region.“Amazon is proud of our cycling culture and to be the lead sponsor of this incredible event that supports our military, engages our workforce, and strengthens communities,” said Steve Hartell, Amazon’s vice president of public policy. “We look forward to continuing to support the Armed Forces Cycling Classic and deepening our commitment to the Capital region.” Amazon partners with Axios to discuss the future of AI in the US workforce Earlier this month, Amazon partnered with Axios Live to host Future of Work: Succeeding in the AI Era at our National Landing campus. Axios senior economics reporter Courtenay Brown and senior tech policy reporter Ashley Gold moderated conversations on the intersectional roles of public-private partnerships, workforce development, and education in the age of Artificial Intelligence.Panelists included:● Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s vice president of worldwide economic development policy● The Honorable Sen. Todd Young● Dr. Anne M. Kress, Northern Virginia Community College PresidentThe discussion explored how policymakers, educators, and the private sector are collaborating to develop training programs that equip workers for new and not-yet defined roles—and how institutions like NOVA are evolving to prepare the next generation for an AI-driven economy.Events like this reflect Amazon’s commitment to convening leaders across sectors to advance real solutions from community college pipelines to federal AI policy. We were proud to host this timely conversation at HQ2 and look forward to bringing more events like this to National Landing. Amazon hosts community book fair at Met Park, unveils Little Library On Saturday, May 16, Amazon hosted a Community Book Fair at Met Park on the campus of HQ2 in National Landing, Virginia. Hundreds of community members came together for a free morning of celebrating books, reading, and math for kids.A bestselling author, free books, and a sweet surpriseNew York Times bestselling author and mathematician Danica McKellar led live readings for children in attendance, and every kid went home with a copy of her book, Goodnight, Numbers. The event also featured crafts, Toby's Ice Cream, and Amazon bananas to fuel the fun.The book fair was produced in collaboration with E3: Elevate Early Education, the READ, VA campaign, and Dolly Parton's Imagination Library of Northern Virginia.A lasting investment in the communityAmazon also announced the installation of a new Little Library on the HQ2 campus, stocked with books from Dolly Parton's Imagination Library. The Little Library will provide free, year-round access to books for children and families in the National Landing community—extending the spirit of the book fair well beyond a single Saturday morning. Amazon and DDOT launch e-cargo bike pilot in Washington, D.C. Amazon is working with the District Department of Transportation (DDOT) to test e-cargo bike deliveries in Washington, D.C.How does the e-cargo bike pilot work?Through the new pilot program, Delivery Service Partners (DSPs) will deliver Amazon packages to customers across the District using a fleet of up to 15 battery-powered e-cargo bikes that move quickly, safely, and quietly through busy city streets.E-cargo bikes are a natural fit for urban deliveries. With a smaller footprint than cars or vans, they can operate in dedicated bike lanes, helping reduce traffic congestion and noise. The e-cargo bikes have four wheels and a secure rear cargo hold, along with covered seating, a windshield, and windshield wipers, providing weather protection for DSP delivery associates.Why is Amazon testing e-cargo bikes in D.C.?\"We've seen the benefits e-cargo bikes bring to urban deliveries around the world, and we're excited to launch this pilot alongside DDOT to help ensure we get it right here in D.C.,\" said Dave Alperson, vice president of North America last mile transportation at Amazon. \"We'll continue working alongside community partners in the National Capital Region who understand the local needs best—combining their insights with our expertise in delivery, technology, and innovation to make a real difference in people's everyday lives.\"Where else does Amazon use e-cargo bikes?Amazon has been investing in micromobility like e-cargo bikes and on-foot deliveries across the globe, including in New York City and more than 50 cities in Europe. The D.C. pilot is the latest chapter in that work, and Amazon looks forward to working closely with DDOT as the company continues to expand safe and efficient deliveries for our neighbors in the National Capital Region. Spring has arrived at Amazon HQ2, and we’re celebrating in full color. This year, Amazon is proud to be part of the National Cherry Blossom Festival, and the celebration has been in full swing across the region. On March 28, Amazon brought the National Mall to life as the presenting sponsor of the Blossom Kite Festival, which brought together families for an afternoon of kite making and live music. Back in National Landing, Pink in the Pool kicked off the season on March 21 with free access to the Long Bridge Aquatics Center and Japanese cultural activities like tea ceremonies, origami, and paper lantern decorating.The best part? There’s still more to come.Pink in the Park returns for its fourth yearAmazon is co-presenting Pink in the Park with the National Landing BID, now in its fourth year as Virginia’s premier celebration of the National Cherry Blossom Festival. The series continues with Pink Beats on April 9 at the Water Park, before coming to a close on April 10 with Art in the Park at Metropolitan Park--an after-hours showcase featuring 50+ local artists, Japanese and pan-Asian cuisine, and DJ entertainment from 5 p.m. to 10 p.m.“Amazon is proud to support Pink in the Park, a vibrant, community-driven tradition that continues to grow each year,” said Patrick Phillippi, head of community engagement for the National Capital Region at Amazon. “We’re excited to welcome neighbors back to Metropolitan Park for the Art of Pink exhibition and to spotlight the creativity of local artists and businesses. It’s a joyful way to celebrate spring and the spirit of this city together.”From the Washington Monument Grounds to Metropolitan Park, Amazon is honored to help bring the Cherry Blossom Festival to life on both sides of the river. Register for Art Park to join us on April 10. Building community together at HQ2 and Virginia 2026 marks a milestone year for Amazon in Virginia. As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in the Commonwealth and approach the opening of Arlington Community High School at Met Park this fall, we’re proud to reflect on how our partnership with Virginia has grown beyond traditional economic development into a model of community-centered investment. Met Park brings jobs, small businesses, and community programmingSince opening Met Park in May 2023, nearly 8,500 Amazon employees now work at our second headquarters. The campus includes 50,000 square feet of retail space for 13 local small businesses and a 2.5-acre public park that has hosted hundreds of community events—from affordable housing summits to farmers markets, live music, and art programming with MoCA Arlington’s Innovation Studio—reaching an estimated 133,000 community members. Since implementing our 5-day return-to-office policy in January 2025, we've continued to see positive economic impacts in the National Landing community, including foot traffic averaged more than 155,000 monthly visitors at Met Park. $1.3 billion investment in affordable housingOur commitment extends far beyond job creation. Through Amazon’s housing fund, we’ve invested $1.3 billion to preserve and create over 10,000 affordable housing units across the National Capital Region. In February, we broke ground on the Victory Center in Alexandria—transforming a long-vacant office building into 377 all-affordable homes. We’ve also donated more than $60 million to 230+ local community partners in the last 12 months. Building Virginia's talent pipelineWe’re building Virginia’s talent pipeline from the ground up, investing more than $5.7 million in CodeVA to expand computer science education and awarding $920,000 in Amazon Future Engineer Scholarships to Virginia students. Over 5,000 Amazon operations employees in Virginia have participated in Career Choice, our pre-paid tuition program.Virginia is a great state to do business. Across the Commonwealth, we continue to invest in our corporate office in Herndon, our fulfillment network including the launch of ORF4—our robotics fulfillment center in Virginia Beach, and in AI and cloud infrastructure that powers businesses statewide. As we enter this milestone year, Amazon remains committed to being a good employer, neighbor, and community partner in Virginia. Amazon breaks ground on 377 all-affordable homes in Alexandria's West End Amazon, Mayor Alyia Gaskins, and developer Stonebridge today broke ground on the Victory Center at 5001 Eisenhower Avenue—a formerly vacant 11-story office building in Alexandria, Virginia, that will be transformed into 377 all-affordable homes by 2027.A building reimaginedWhen this project was first conceived, it was planned as a mixed-affordability development. Amazon's investment changed that. Today, every one of the 377 homes will be affordable—235 of them two- and three-bedroom units for families—with half available to renters earning 50% to 60% of area median income and half to those earning 80% of area median income. Amazon's participation also extended the affordability commitment from 40 years to 99 years. \"This project represents something bigger than just one building,\" said Steve Hartell, vice president of public policy at Amazon. \"It will help chart a course for a new future—not only for the generations of residents who will call this place home, but for the broader Eisenhower West redevelopment as well.\"Built for Alexandria's essential workersWith 235 family-sized units and easy Metro access, 5001 Eisenhower Avenue is designed for Alexandria's essential workers and families who want to stay in the neighborhoods they love. \"I am proud to be the leader of a city where we have been so tirelessly committed to building affordable housing,\" said Mayor Alyia Gaskins of Alexandria, Virginia. \"Making sure that we are building housing options everywhere and in every part of our community so that anyone who wants to live here is able to live here and stay here.\"Part of a larger commitmentToday's groundbreaking is part of Amazon's commitment of more than $1.3 billion to create and preserve over 10,000 affordable homes in the National Capital Region. In Alexandria, Amazon has committed over $166 million to support more than 1,400 affordable homes—part of our $3.6 billion Housing Fund spanning the Puget Sound region, National Capital Region, and Nashville. Amazon supports Fairfax County students in Presidential AI Challenge Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) students showcased innovative AI solutions this week as part of the Seize the Moment Student AI Innovation Challenge, a program supported by Amazon through its work with Playlab.Top student teams from 25 FCPS high schools demonstrated custom AI apps at Herndon High School, just miles from Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, Virginia. Students used Playlab's sandboxed AI environment to create AI-powered apps designed to tackle real-world challenges, including improving government policies and helping international students transition.The evening concluded with five finalist teams presenting their apps to a panel of judges from Amazon, FCPS, and the Virginia Department of Education.\"It's inspiring to see students across Fairfax County and throughout the country build innovative solutions to address challenges in their classrooms and communities,\" said Valerie Singer, general manager of global education at Amazon Web Services. \"Amazon is proud to support STEM education for students of all ages, and we're excited to partner with Playlab on the Presidential AI Challenge. Together, we're helping students safely use AI to learn and create.\"This effort is part of Amazon's support for AI education and literacy for learners and workers. This broad work includes support for the Presidential AI Challenge and the Pledge to America's Youth, as well as Amazon's Future 2030—a $2.5 billion commitment to expand access to education and skills training to help prepare at least 50 million people for the future of work. Amazon joins National Innovation Quarter as founding partner in National Landing Amazon is proud to call National Landing home. With its cutting-edge workforce, diverse and growing suite of industries and amenities, and proximity to federal policymakers and global institutions, it is no surprise that National Landing is a driver of regional economic growth. Since establishing HQ2, we have seen how bringing leaders together in the right place generates breakthroughs in national competitiveness and technology.Building innovation beyond HQ2Our efforts to build the future don’t stop at HQ2. We are investing in community organizations and programs that strengthen collaboration and tackle some of the most pressing issues facing our communities. In National Landing, this commitment includes our role as a Founding Partner for the new National Innovation Quarter—National IQ.Launched this month, National IQ is a landmark nonprofit organization that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and entrepreneurship to amplify the area’s innovation ecosystem.Industry leaders join forces in National LandingBy tapping into the public and private sector organizations across Crystal City, Pentagon City, and Potomac Yard, National IQ will strengthen collaboration and solidify the community as a global innovation hub. The impact of this initiative will be felt immediately with the rollout of year-round programming, initiatives, and startup and innovation accelerators.Marked by a launch event at the National Landing Experience Center on February 11, Amazon joined other Founding Partners and early sponsors to celebrate National IQ’s role as a catalyst for innovation and growth for the region and the nation.“National IQ creates a powerful platform to accelerate this innovation ecosystem,” said Brian Stout, senior manager of U.S. public policy at Amazon. “It brings the right people together, with the right resources, in the right place, and that combination is what drives breakthrough solutions. We’re excited to see the breakthroughs that emerge when this community comes together.” Amazon celebrates HQ2 community impact: $60 million donated to more than 230 local partners in 2025 In December, Amazon employees gathered at the company’s Amazon Visitor Landing in HQ2 for a celebration of community impact.Brian Huseman, Amazon’s vice president of public policy and community engagement, welcomed familiar faces and new colleagues alike to reflect on the ways Amazon is investing in the region. “You’re the ones volunteering at local schools, mentoring students, supporting food banks, and showing up for our community partners.” Huseman told Amazon employees. “You understand what it means to be a good neighbor here. And you’re our best ambassadors—the people who can authentically share Amazon’s story because you are part of it.”Tracy Sayegh Gabriel, chief executive officer of the National Landing Business Improvement District, moderated a conversation with Holly Sullivan, vice president of worldwide economic development policy, and Brian Kenner, global head of geographic giving and community engagement. The conversation explored Amazon’s economic contributions in Virginia and hyperlocal giving strategy that has made HQ2 a model for community investment.With 8,500 employees at HQ2 and over 66,000 across Virginia, Maryland, and Washington, D.C., Amazon is creating meaningful economic and community benefits. In 2025 alone, Amazon donated over $60 million to more than 230 local community partners throughout the DMV.A Q&A panel featuring Sullivan, Kenner, Senthil Sankaran (managing principal of Amazon’s housing fund), and Bettina Stix (global director of Amazon Community Impact) highlighted the company’s comprehensive approach in the region: the creation and preservation of more than 10,000 affordable homes supporting more than 22,000 residents, more than 1.5 million meals delivered to local organizations and families, and more than 700,000 student hours of free STEM education provided.Amazon employees are at the heart of this work. In 2025, more than 2,200 HQ2 employees participated in more than 270 volunteer events, supporting over 100 community organizations. This work represents Amazon’s Leadership Principles in action, demonstrating that success and scale bring broad responsibility to the communities we call home.Read more about Amazon’s community impact in the National Capital Region. Holiday magic at HQ2: Art, community, and local flavor at Met Park The holiday season is in full swing at HQ2, and Metropolitan Park is becoming a destination where art, local businesses, and community experiences come together. These moments reflect the spirit of what we are building in National Landing and the partnerships that continue to grow around our campus.From December 1 through January 25, visitors can explore Horizon, an immersive pop-up art installation by Creos and artist Olivier Landreville. The experience features five egg-shaped cocoons designed to create a sense of calm. Guests can sit back, gently rock, and look up into starry skies, bird formations, or a soothing leafy canopy. Soft lighting, natural wood textures, and layered soundscapes create a moment of quiet in the heart of HQ2. Horizon adds to Amazon's commitment to supporting public art and cultural programming that builds community at Met Park.On December 6, the National Landing BID, in partnership with Amazon, brings The Lodge to Met Park with a full lineup of seasonal activities that also spotlight local businesses and artists. Neighbors, families, and four-legged friends can enjoy gingerbread chalet building inside the MoCA Arlington Innovation Studio, along with selfies with Santa, face painting, letters to Santa, ornament making, and Ski Vacation Bingo under covered tents on the Central Green. Visitors can also take part in the Peppermint Mocha Latte Competition which features local coffee shops—including Good Company Coffee, one of several small businesses supported through HQ2's engagement and vendor partnership efforts.These collaborations with the BID and our neighborhood retailers help activate Met Park, create economic opportunities, and reinforce HQ2 as a welcoming, community-centered destination. Cloud Connect brings university talent to HQ2 for hands-on learning and mentorship On November 14, Amazon's Black Employee Network (BEN Tech) partnered with Amazon University Talent Acquisition and Field North America Tech Customer Service teams to welcome more than 120 university students to HQ2 for Cloud Connect. The event gave students an inside look at Amazon while helping them connect their academic interests to real career paths.Students attended from across the region, representing more than 10 colleges and universities including Howard University, University of Maryland, University of Virginia, Virginia Tech, George Mason University, Morgan State, and Carnegie Mellon. Their studies covered more than 15 majors such as computer science, cybersecurity, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and business. Student organizations were also represented, including the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), Society of Women Engineers (SWE), ColorStack, and the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE).The day began with a panel featuring senior leaders who shared the turns their careers have taken and the impact their teams have on the technology that powers Amazon. Their stories opened the door for meaningful conversations and set the tone for the hands-on learning ahead.Following the senior leader panel, students joined a careers in tech conversation and a recruiting workshop before heading into the PartyRock workshop. There, they built generative AI applications in teams and presented them in a Shark Tank style pitch session. The room quickly shifted into a creative space filled with notes, sketches, and teammates working together to bring their concepts to life.To keep that momentum going, each student left paired with a mentor who will support them as they navigate school, internships, and early career decisions. More than 65 Amazon volunteers helped create an environment where students and employees could connect and learn from one another.Cloud Connect not only introduced students to opportunities at Amazon, it built relationships that strengthen the connection between Amazon teams and the communities we serve. Amazon and Horning Partners open Plaza Towers, adding 288 affordable homes to National Capital Region When Plaza Towers opened its doors this week in Hyattsville, Maryland, it brought hundreds of affordable apartments to Prince George's County, and what makes this even more impactful is that they will remain affordable for generations to come.The 288-unit development, built by Horning Partners with $18.72 million in low-cost financing from Amazon, held its ribbon-cutting ceremony with community members and local officials. For many attendees, the building represents more than new construction—it's a chance for families to stay in a region where housing costs have pushed many out.\"We created our housing fund for projects just like this,\" said Senthil Sankaran, managing principal of Amazon's housing fund. \"Working alongside partners like Horning and County Executive Braveboy, we're helping more families access housing they can afford.\"The location offers residents easy access to transit lines, employment centers, and community resources—factors that can make the difference between struggling and thriving for families on tight budgets.Plaza Towers is part of Amazon's broader effort in the National Capital Region, where the company has committed over $1.3 billion to help preserve and create more than 10,000 affordable homes. Other projects include the Somos community at McLean Metro, The Chimes in North Bethesda, and sustainable housing developments in Washington, D.C. Together, these initiatives aim to benefit more than 22,000 residents across the region.As housing affordability continues to challenge communities nationwide, developments like Plaza Towers show what's possible when developers, local government, and private partners align their resources toward a common goal. Amazon glows green to honor veterans through Operation Green Light This week, Amazon sites across the country are glowing green—a simple yet powerful symbol of appreciation for veterans, active-duty service members, military families, and those who support them.As part of the fifth annual Operation Green Light for Veterans, led by the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO), Amazon is illuminating more than 200 sites and buildings nationwide from November 4–11. The initiative honors the contributions of veterans and their families and shines a light on the importance of gratitude in our communities.\"Operation Green Light is an opportunity to thank veterans for both their service to our nation and their contributions to our communities,\" said NACo Executive Director Matthew Chase. \"With hundreds of counties coming together to light up everything from courthouses to bridges, we hope veterans will see and feel our gratitude for their selflessness and courage. We thank Amazon for their continued support and partnership in helping honor and uplift those who have served.\"At HQ2, this effort reflects Amazon's commitment to being a good employer and neighbor. With more than 6,500 members across the D.C., Maryland, and Virginia area, our employee-led group Warriors at Amazon embodies the spirit of service—supporting one another and giving back long after their time in uniform.\"What makes Operation Green Light powerful isn't just the color, it's the connection. Across more than 600 chapters and 20,000 members globally, Amazon's Warriors network continues to unite Amazon employees who lead with gratitude and show up with purpose. This moment is a thank-you to every veteran, family member, and ally who stands in support of the military community, today and every day,\" said David Sopczak, president of Warriors at Amazon.To our veterans, service members, and the Warriors at Amazon community—thank you for your courage, leadership, and the light you bring to Amazon every day. Amazon provides an additional $1.5 million to support National Capital Region food banks Amazon is dedicated to ensuring communities have reliable access to food year-round. As part of our overall commitment, we’ve donated an additional $1.5 million+ to help local food banks in the Northern Virginia and Washington D.C. metro area to meet increased demand. Throughout 2025, we’ve also used our transportation network to deliver more than 1.5 million meals to local organizations tackling food insecurity and have donated more than 2.9 million meals to help those experiencing hunger in the National Capitol Region.\"With recent demand on local food banks reaching new levels, supporting reliable access to nutrition has never been more critical,” said Bettina Stix, Amazon’s global director of community impact. “By combining these donations with Amazon’s free home delivery and bulk delivery services offered to local food banks, we’re helping our partners meet immediate needs while also strengthening the region’s food system.”The contributions will support key regional organizations addressing food insecurity, including Capital Area Food Bank, Bread for the City, Arlington Food Assistance Center, DC Central Kitchen, Martha's Table, and So Others May Eat. These funds will help provide thousands of families with nutritious meals, holiday food assistance, and year-round hunger relief services.“Amazon’s contribution to our Holiday Helpings will provide over 18,500 local families with turkeys and other trimmings for the Thanksgiving holiday,” said Ashley Domm, Chief Development Officer, Bread for the City. “We originally planned to support 15,000 families this year, but because of generosity like Amazon's, we were able to go the extra mile. We are happy that Amazon was able to again provide this for our community this year.”Amazon helps food banks reach more people using our established last-mile infrastructure and logistics expertise. On top of financial donations, our delivery network—including Delivery Service Partners, Amazon Flex, and Amazon Shipping—has delivered more than 60 million meals directly to over 200,000 families experiencing food insecurity across the United States since 2020. We also provide bulk transportation services for free, moving tens of thousands of pounds of food weekly between suppliers, food banks, and community distribution points using our fleet of vehicles. In addition, our grocery stores provide excess food donations to food banks around the region. Together, these efforts help ensure food reaches families in need regardless of transportation costs, barriers, or mobility challenges.Learn more about Amazon’s commitment to food security. Amazon welcomes DC Startup & Tech Week 2025 at HQ2 Amazon proudly partnered with DC Startup & Tech Week (DCSTW) and Arlington Economic Development (AED) to celebrate the special tenth anniversary of this influential conference, hosting a full day of programming at HQ2. DCSTW has been a catalyst for regional innovation, creating vital connections between investors, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders across the DMV area and beyond.On Thursday, October 23, Amazon HQ2 hosted a full day of programming focused on innovation, entrepreneurship, and the future of work. As a leading sponsor alongside AED, Amazon facilitated sessions that strengthened ties with the region’s top innovators and local organizations.“We’re thrilled to partner with DC Startup & Tech Week,” said Patrick Phillippi, Head of Community Engagement for Greater Washington at Amazon. “Hosting the region’s leading founders and innovators at Amazon’s HQ2 reflects our deep commitment to empowering local entrepreneurship, fostering collaboration, and shaping the next chapter of innovation in the DMV.”HQ2 Event highlights included:An exclusive networking session featuring 10 local startups pitching to investorsFounder workshops and investment discussionsPolicy conversations led by Arlington Economic Development Director Ryan TouhillSessions exploring AI, automation, and emerging technologiesInnovation spotlights celebrating women leaders driving change in techThroughout the day, discussions centered on artificial intelligence, automation, and the rapidly evolving technology landscape. Attendees engaged in dynamic fireside chats and policy conversations that highlighted the region’s growing startup ecosystem.This partnership exemplifies Amazon's commitment to investing in local communities—supporting founders, fostering innovation, and helping entrepreneurs transform ideas into impact. Celebrating Hispanic Heritage Month at HQ2: a season of music, flavor, and cultura At Amazon HQ2, Hispanic Heritage Month wasn’t just celebrated, it was lived. From lively evenings at Metropolitan Park to cultural showcases inside HQ2, each moment reflected the energy, connection, and creativity that define the Latino community across the DMV. Together with local partners, employees, and neighbors, Amazon spent the season amplifying voices, sharing stories, and honoring traditions that continue to shape our region.Every Thursday this fall, Metropolitan Park transformed into a vibrant gathering place for NaLa Noches, a Latin music series sponsored by Amazon and the National Landing Business Improvement District. Families, neighbors, and Amazonians came together as the park pulsed with rhythm and color, filled with boleros, salsa, and Venezuelan jazz from artists like Miramar, Alfredo Mojica, and Bio Ritmo. Guests enjoyed tacos and tres leches cake from local favorite Taqueria Xochi, along with Topo Chico Sabores, while the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington led hands-on art activities inspired by Día de los Muertos. The series captured what makes National Landing special—people from all walks of life gathering to celebrate culture and community.That same spirit carried inside HQ2, where the DMV Latinos employee-led group hosted Taste of Latin Cultura III, transforming the space into a celebration of music, flavor, and belonging. Amazonians heard from HQ2-based Latino leaders—including Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President of Public Policy, and Gonzalo de Dios, Head of Licensing & International Regulatory Affairs at Project Kuiper—alongside Michel Zajur, CEO of the Virginia Hispanic Chamber of Commerce, who spoke on representation, entrepreneurship, and culture’s role in innovation.The celebration continued through Expressions and Compass culinary workshops with Executive Chef George Gomez, where employees explored Latin flavors. The season may have ended, but the rhythm remains, in the community we support and the spaces it creates for cultures to shine. AWS Skills Center Arlington celebrates 3 years of building a skilled HQ2 community Just steps away from Amazon’s HQ2 campus in National Landing, the AWS Skills Center Arlington is celebrating a major milestone: three years of empowering learners, growing the region’s tech workforce, and building community.Since opening in 2022, the AWS Skills Center has become a flagship investment in workforce development for HQ2, welcoming thousands of visitors and providing free cloud computing training to learners of all backgrounds—no prior experience required. Its mission aligns closely with Amazon’s broader commitment to making HQ2 not just a place to work, but a hub for economic opportunity and community impact across the DMV region.On October 23, 2025, the AWS Skills Center will host a full day of free, public events at 1550 – G Crystal Drive in Arlington, inviting the community to celebrate this milestone together.The day begins with “Give Back Together,” a community service project assembling hygiene kits for local families in need. Later in the afternoon, a “Vision & Voices” panel will feature distinguished organizations reflecting on the center’s journey and exploring future workforce development initiatives.The celebration continues later in the day with an inspiring learner panel, networking with industry professionals, delicious food, and exciting giveaways.As Amazon continues to grow its HQ2 presence, the AWS Skills Center plays a critical role in building a pipeline of local talent and expanding access to tech skills, ensuring that the opportunities created here benefit the entire community.The event is free and open to everyone. Join us as we celebrate three years of learning, impact, and community-building at HQ2’s doorstep.Register now on the site. Amazon HQ2 hosts Uniting Women in Cyber 2025 Amazon HQ2 recently welcomed more than 250 leaders from government, industry, and the cybersecurity community to the WAS17 Event Center for Uniting Women in Cyber (UWIC) 2025. As a lead sponsor, Amazon was proud to provide a platform for experts to connect—through both relationships and technology, to strengthen global cyber preparedness and resilience.Throughout the day, participants engaged in deep technical discussions and thoughtful policy conversations on how to protect nations, organizations, and communities from evolving cyber threats. This year’s conference placed a spotlight on senior executives leading cybersecurity innovation across critical sectors.Amazon's Hart Rossman, vice president of Global Security Services at AWS, spoke on a panel that discussed cybersecurity collaboration in a globally interconnected economy.“AWS revolutionized computing with the invention of the world’s first public cloud nearly two decades ago. Today, millions of users build their businesses on our secure and compliant cloud and AI services, making us an integral part of the global supply chain. I was energized to connect with other leaders at this event and discuss how AWS’s Shared Responsibility Model and innovative cybersecurity strategies are transforming cloud security at global scale,” said Rossman.Amazon is deeply committed to empowering women in technology and advancing careers in cybersecurity. Through comprehensive internal programs, targeted mentorship opportunities, and initiatives led by our Women at Amazon employee-led group, we’re fostering an environment where women can thrive in technical roles and leadership positions.Located in the heart of the DMV, HQ2 continues to serve as a hub where public and private sector leaders come together to build partnerships that strengthen communities and drive progress. By partnering with UWIC, Amazon is helping to elevate diverse voices, spark meaningful collaboration, and advance cybersecurity innovation—reflecting our ongoing mission to be a good neighbor and employer in the region. Amazon facility and employees near HQ2 recognized by Virginia Department for the Blind and Vision Impaired This National Disability Employment Awareness Month, an Amazon operations facility in Northern Virginia near the companies HQ2 offices was honored as Employer of the Year from the Virginia Department for the Blind and Vision Impaired (DBVI), highlighting Amazon’s ongoing efforts to be the earth’s most inclusive and accessible employer for people with disabilities.During a celebration event on Wednesday, October 8, the DBVI recognized Amazon’s RMN3 operations facility in Stafford, Virginia for the accommodations the facility’s team has made to empower blind and vision impaired employees. The recognition underscores Amazon’s commitment to building a diverse and inclusive workforce across the Northern Virginia region, where HQ2 is helping drive job creation and opportunity.At the same event, four of RMN3’s visually impaired employees, Mavis Williams, Alice Bickersteth, Antonette Johnson, Nacel Johnson, were also honored as the DBVI’s Employees of the Year. They discussed the positive impact Amazon’s efforts have on their livelihoods and how their jobs have afforded them to ability to lead independent lives.Amazon is proud to be named the DBVI’s Employer of the Year and is committed to investing in its employees in Northern Virginia and providing a safe and welcoming workplace. Amazon celebrates new STEM program and educational opportunities in southern Arlington Nearly three years into our partnership with Aspire, Amazon is proud to support the grand opening of Aspire's STEM Makerspace.On September 30, Amazon joined community leaders, educators, students, and families to celebrate the official opening of Aspire Afterschool Learning's Makerspace. The first no-cost community space for STEM programming in the area, the makerspace was created in response to the needs and input of the community.The new space expands STEM programming and hands-on learning opportunities, enables Aspire to support additional students in its programs, and provides a central meeting place for Aspire staff, students, and families.Amazon provided the initial funding for the project to enable Aspire to expand its facility and add the makerspace. With the new space, Aspire is incorporating science labs, coding classes, video and design projects, robotics demonstrations, structured play, and other interactive activities into its programming.Aspire served a record number of students during its 2024-2025 programs, with an average of 72% improving their math levels over the past two years. With community support, Aspire has sustained a 119% increase in enrollment over the last two years — in addition to expanding its fall STEM programming.\"Amazon is honored to support Aspire's work to ensure that all children have the support they need from family, school, and the community,\" said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement at Amazon.With the opening of the Makerspace, Aspire is taking another step to bridge the \"imagination gap\" for historically underserved 3rd-8th-grade Arlington students through exposure to STEM fields. Amazon's Black Employee Network builds community at HQ2 At Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, the Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) is bringing our good neighbor mission to life—one event, one partnership, and one young leader at a time.
