This year, BEN-DMV partnered with Together at Amazon for their second annual Day of Service, joining forces with Everybody Wins DC (EWDC), The Distant Relatives Project, and Cardz for Kidz to promote youth literacy and support those experiencing homelessness. The event united more than 200 HQ2 volunteers for a day of service and impact. Volunteers assembled 1,000 hygiene kits, packed more than 500 books with personal notes to share with local schools, and created more than 600 cards for hospitalized children. \"Representation through service means creating spaces where our communities can see, hear, and feel that they matter. Giving back locally is how we carry that forward, and the Tech Day of Service reflects the legacy we are building, one grounded in visibility, voice, and lasting impact,\" said Tyson J. Thompkins, director community engagement of BEN-DMV. BEN-DMV's impact goes far beyond one event. From cultural celebrations to neighborhood cleanups, the group builds programs that reflect HQ2's vision: a hub of innovation and inclusion that strengthens community. So far this year, they've hosted 59 events, partnered with more than 40 organizations and businesses, and contributed more than 3,600 volunteer hours—all while creating space for employees to lead with purpose and pride. \"We're not just redefining what employee care looks like in the workplace—we're demonstrating how that same commitment can fuel meaningful change in the world around us,\" said Cash Ashley, president of BEN-DMV. As HQ2 grows, BEN-DMV is helping define what it means to show up for neighbors—with heart, culture, and intention. Community shines at Amazon HQ2's 2025 Community Day Met Park came alive over the weekend as Amazon's third annual HQ2 Community Day transformed our Arlington neighborhood into a vibrant festival of connection and creativity. The event wasn't just a celebration; it was our heartfelt thank-you note to the community for welcoming Amazon to the neighborhood. Imagine the scene: Children bouncing through a giant foam pit, their laughter mixing with the beats of a live DJ, while parents watched their little ones become temporary artists at the LEGO wall and face-painting stations. The air was sweet with cotton candy and filled with thousands of bubbles dancing in the autumn breeze.The Think Big Experience brought AWS technology to life, turning complex concepts into wonder. Young minds were captivated as they controlled robots, wrote their first lines of code, and marveled at The Science Guys of Baltimore's spectacular shows. It was science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) education disguised as entertainment!Food enthusiasts found their paradise at the Eat Loco Farmers Market and Met Park food venues, where local flavors took center stage. As families lounged on picnic blankets enjoying discounted treats, the festival atmosphere was perfect for an afternoon of community bonding.Amazon Alley showcased local entrepreneurship, featuring DMV-based businesses like Scarlettwares, Mocktail Club, and Bon AppeSweet. These innovative small business owners reminded us why our region's creative spirit is second to none.We are grateful to everyone who joined us and look forward to many more moments together. Arlington Community High School breaks ground at HQ2's Metropolitan Park On September 12, Amazon stood alongside Arlington Public Schools, county leaders, educators, and community partners to celebrate a milestone decade in the making—the unveiling of Arlington Community High School's (ACHS) future home at HQ2's Metropolitan Park. Starting in 2026, ACHS will finally have a home after years of operating without one.The celebration began with a warm reception catered by local restaurant Peruvian Brothers, setting the stage for an afternoon filled with community spirit. Leaders reflected on the significance of this moment—not only for ACHS students, but for Arlington as a whole.ACHS currently serves more than 300 students, many of whom balance work and family responsibilities while pursuing their high school diplomas. Their future home at HQ2 will offer more than classrooms. \"This home is more than just a building,\" said Jeannette Allen, principal of Arlington Community High School. \"It is a place where our students can feel stability, belonging and pride. It is a place where we can deepen our roots, strengthen our programs and build lasting partnerships with Amazon and this amazing and thriving business and education community.\"For Amazon, the ACHS partnership builds on a long-standing investment in the region. Since announcing Arlington as the site of HQ2, the company has contributed more than $239 million to local nonprofits, schools, and organizations.\"When we chose Arlington as our home for our second headquarters, we committed to being more than just a neighbor—we wanted to be a true community partner,\" said Stephen Hartell, vp of public policy at Amazon. \"We couldn't be more thrilled to welcome Arlington Community High School students into the heart of our HQ2 campus. This partnership represents exactly what we envisioned with HQ2—creating meaningful connections that strengthen our community.\"The unveiling of ACHS's new home is more than a building announcement—it's the next chapter in a story about resilience, opportunity, and the power of community. Together, Amazon and Arlington are proving that strong schools build strong communities. Amazon HQ2 serves up sweet summer fun with Banana Split Day On Monday, August 25, 2025, in Arlington, Amazon HQ2 brought neighbors together for one of the sweetest traditions of the summer—Banana Split Day at Metropolitan Park.The community gathered around Amazon's iconic banana stand to enjoy free banana splits, crafted with local favorite Toby's Homemade Ice Cream, complete with all the toppings to make each scoop a masterpiece. Live music from talented local performers filled the park, and the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA) hosted creative, family-friendly activities that kept the afternoon lively and colorful.Banana Split Day is more than a summertime treat, it's part of Amazon HQ2's commitment to being a good neighbor, fostering community connection, and supporting local businesses and cultural organizations in National Landing. Partnerships with small businesses like Toby's and nonprofits like MoCA reflect our dedication to creating moments that bring people together while contributing to the local economy and arts scene.The event continued a tradition that began at Amazon's Seattle headquarters and has now become an HQ2 favorite. Judging by the smiles, the laughter, and the empty banana peels, it's safe to say this year's celebration was a hit. Toby's gave out 1,229 scoops to employees and community members, with over 300 served within the first hour!Thank you to our partners, neighbors, and everyone who joined us to make Banana Split Day 2025 a celebration to remember. We look forward to welcoming you back to Metropolitan Park for more events that connect, inspire, and celebrate our Arlington community. Amazon employees 'Stuff the Bus' with Salvation Army to support students Last month, Amazon HQ2 joined WJLA-TV and The Salvation Army for the annual Stuff the Bus back-to-school drive—united by one goal: helping local students start the school year ready to succeed.The event was a great success. More than 2,100 items were collected, with many community members also making online donations through 7News' Amazon Wish List. \"We've been here three years now since we opened our headquarters. We have really made a commitment to be a good neighbor and a good partner to this region,\" said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement.Amazon's momentum in Northern Virginia continues to grow. With more than 8,300 employees now based at HQ2, leaders underscored the company's dedication to investing in the community. \"It's important for us to be involved here. We've got 8,300 employees at this beautiful facility, and we've been part of the community from day one,\" said Stephen Hartell, vice president of U.S. public policy.Partnering with The Salvation Army reflects Amazon's commitment to long-term impact. Stuff the Bus goes beyond backpacks and school supplies—it's an investment in children's education, their confidence, and the future of the region.We are grateful to everyone who donated, volunteered, and spread the word. Your generosity shows how Amazon HQ2's commitment to strengthening community comes alive through meaningful action. Together, we're ensuring that students and families start the school year with the tools they need to thrive. Amazon teams up with NFL, Washington Commanders, and Smithsonian for STEM education initiative The National Air and Space Museum set the stage for an evening where sports, science, and technology intersected to create a unique learning experience for local students. As part of Amazon's Thursday Night Football for Good initiative, the company joined forces with the Washington Commanders, NFL, and the Smithsonian to host an innovative STEM education event for Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington members. The event, part of the TNF for Good initiative, showed how sports can be a powerful catalyst for educational engagement. Local students from the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington received books and STEM kits to continue their educational journey beyond the museum walls. The event is part of a broader initiative to distribute 1 million books and educational resources to children across all 30 NFL markets this year. \"Through Amazon's TNF for Good initiative, we're not just distributing books—we're distributing dreams, possibilities, to empower future leaders,\" said Brian Kenner, director of community engagement, Amazon. The joint effort represents a shared commitment to education and opportunity in the National Capital Region, where Amazon's HQ2 has become an active community partner. By bringing together the Smithsonian's educational resources, the NFL's Inspire Change program, and the Boys & Girls Clubs' community connections, we're creating opportunities for local youth to engage with STEM in new ways. This powerful partnership between cultural institutions, sports organizations, corporate entities, and community groups demonstrates what's possible when diverse organizations unite behind a common goal. The evening stood as testament to the impact that collaborative community engagement can have in inspiring the next generation of explorers, inventors, and innovators. Opening doors to the next generation of builders at HQ2 In 2024 a small idea took root: How can Amazon open our doors to inspire the next generation of builders? That vision came to life when iD Tech, in partnership with ProServe, AWS Security and the Inclusive eXperiences and Technology team, launched the inaugural HQ2 Summer Camp, welcoming Amazon employees and local youth into a world of hands-on STEM learning.The two-week program offered a timely solution for Amazon builders balancing work and family schedules during the summer. HQ2 transformed into a buzzing hub of creativity where students aged 7–17 explored game design, artificial intelligence, Python programming, and robotics. Campers also toured our HQ2 offices, joined leadership for career chats, and expressed their creativity through Amazon’s “Expression Lab” projects. Mornings focused on coding, robotics development, and game design. Afternoons brought team-building games, social activities, and robotics competitions.What made the camp unique was the direct access to Amazon tech leaders who shared real-world insights, showing how harnessing curiosity can inspire innovation with world changing impact. By the end, campers had built functional games, AI models, and robotics prototypes, leaving with project files, learning transcripts, new skills, confidence, and an ignited passion for technology.For Amazon employees, the camp was a way to share their workplace with their families. “My child loved every minute, and I appreciated being able to share part of my day with them,” one parent said. A camper called HQ2 “the best office in the whole world.”The program advances Amazon’s mission to foster innovation, strengthen local partnerships, and develop future talent. Through this initiative, Amazon continues its commitment to support employees' families, enrich our community, and inspire the next generation of innovators. Amazon's employee jazz ensemble hits all the right notes at HQ2 At Amazon's HQ2, culture isn't just curated, it's created by our incredible employees who bring it to life every day. Recently, that spirit hit a high note as HQBlue, an eight-member jazz band made up entirely of Amazon HQ2 employees, delivered a soulful lunchtime performance at Met Park featuring classics like \"Fly Me to the Moon\" and \"Autumn Leaves.\" What began as casual jam sessions among coworkers quickly grew into something more. The group, made up of engineers and legal professionals, rehearsed during lunch breaks and after hours, building harmony across teams and titles. Their debut drew a crowd of more than 100 Amazon employees, turning an ordinary afternoon into a celebration of creativity, connection, and soul.Moments like this showcase what makes HQ2 more than just an office. It's a community where people are encouraged to bring their full selves to work, including their hidden talents. Whether launching a product or playing jazz, employees are shaping a workplace where multidimensional talent is welcomed and celebrated.As Amazon deepens its roots in Arlington, Virginia, it remains committed to building a vibrant environment that fosters collaboration, joy, and spontaneous connection. HQBlue is just one example of how return-to-office at HQ2 isn't about going back, it's about moving forward together.At HQ2, employees' passions don't have to stay after hours. They can take the stage. Amazon’s Global Month of Volunteering at HQ2 At Amazon, we believe being a good neighbor means showing up for the communities where we live and work. This commitment comes to life each year during our Global Month of Volunteering—a company-wide effort to support the people and places that surround us.This May, more than 2,200 employees in HQ2 joined forces to support more than 100 local nonprofits and community partners across Arlington, Northern Virginia, and Washington, D.C. From Met Park to National Landing, teams came together to plant gardens, pack essential supplies, clean local parks, and build meaningful connections with their neighbors.Whether it was new hires teaming up with long-time Amazon employees, or employees from different departments working side-by-side for the first time, one thing was clear: service creates community.These moments of impact, both big and small, left a lasting impression. Many of the organizations we worked with shared how much they appreciated the energy and commitment HQ2 volunteers brought to each project.While Global Month of Volunteering may be over, our commitment to the community continues year-round. HQ2 is home to people who care deeply about their colleagues, their neighbors, and the future of the region we all share.Thank you to everyone who gave their time and energy this month. Your efforts remind us that volunteering isn’t just a one-time event, it’s a cornerstone of what it means to be part of HQ2. Warriors@Amazon: Service, community, and commitment at HQ2 At Amazon's HQ2, being a good neighbor means showing up with humility, consistency, and purpose. One powerful example of this commitment is Warriors@Amazon, an employee-led group made up of veterans, military spouses, and allies who are using their shared dedication to service to strengthen ties with the local community.Whether through volunteerism, education, or support for those in need, Warriors@Amazon is helping to shape Amazon's presence in the National Capital Region in a way that honors the past while investing in the future.The National Memorial Day parade celebrationThis May, Warriors@Amazon proudly participated in the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., as part of Amazon's Global Month of Volunteering. The event, one of the nation's most visible tributes to fallen service members, offered a meaningful opportunity for HQ2 employees to honor those who served and continue to serve.For many in the group, it was a deeply personal experience, one that reflected both Amazon's support of veterans and its role as a community partner in the region.Growing for good: Urban agriculture at Arlington HouseJust minutes from HQ2's offices, a quiet act of service is taking root. Each month from spring through fall, Warriors@Amazon volunteers and partners with the Capital Region Sustainability chapter to tend the kitchen garden at Arlington House, part of Arlington National Cemetery. In collaboration with the National Park Service and Plot Against Hunger, the produce harvested here is donated to local food banks, supporting neighbors across Northern Virginia.This project blends history, sustainability, and community service. Dozens of Amazon employees—many with military connections—volunteer their time to grow food that helps nourish families while preserving one of the area's most meaningful landmarks.Building community, togetherFrom participating in the Race Across America to supporting local veteran-focused initiatives, Warriors@Amazon's efforts reflect Amazon HQ2's broader vision: to be a responsible and engaged community partner.As HQ2 continues to grow, Amazon remains focused on investing in the region—not just through jobs and infrastructure, but through people, relationships, and long-term community impact.Warriors@Amazon embodies that mission. Their work is consistent, compassionate, and deeply connected to the communities Amazon serves. Through groups like Warriors@Amazon, HQ2 is building more than a physical presence, it's building trust, connection, and shared purpose. Together, we're growing stronger, one act of service at a time. Supporting Virginia Tech’s creativity and innovation at HQ2 At Amazon, we’re proud to support Virginia Tech’s Institute for Creativity, Arts, and Technology (ICAT) as they bring innovative, creative experiences to life. This year, we launched a partnership with the institute to provide opportunities for collaboration, offering our Expressions Studio space at Met Park to host workshops for employees and community members alike—showcasing how creativity and technology can thrive together.Since August 2024, we’ve hosted workshops for more than 100 employees and community members. Participants engaged in hands-on sessions designed to spark creativity and encourage fresh approaches to problem-solving. These experiences allowed them to step away from their daily routines and explore new ways of thinking—transforming food waste into art and discovering other unique forms of creative expression.Beyond the workshops, the program included a visit to the Torpedo Factory Art Center, a renowned local venue known for its emphasis on innovation and artistic collaboration. By facilitating this visit, we helped further connect employees with the local art scene—reinforcing the idea that creativity is a vital component of innovation.Amazon’s HQ2 thrives on our commitment to fostering a culture of creativity and community. While Virginia Tech leads the way, we’re proud to be a supportive partner in bringing these ideas to life—helping to build an environment where both technology and the arts can flourish.Through partnerships like this, we’re supporting initiatives that push boundaries, inspire new ideas, and enrich the broader community. It’s just one example of how Amazon HQ2 works behind the scenes to amplify creative efforts that shape a brighter, more innovative future. Leadership Spotlight: Amazonians driving innovation and growth at HQ2 HQ2 is a true headquarters for Amazon and a wide array of business units and teams work out of Met Park. Our more than 8,000 employees span departments including Prime Video, Public Policy, Amazon Web Services (AWS), HR, and more. They each contribute unique perspectives that shape the company’s future.Here’s a look at a few leaders driving Amazon’s vision forward:Francessca Vasquez, Vice President, AWS Professional Services and Generative AI Innovation CenterA technology leader with over 25 years of industry experience, Vasquez currently drives AWS's global consulting and product services across 48 countries, managing P&L and customer engagement for public sector, commercial, and partner operations. She excels in guiding organizations through generative AI transformations, leveraging AWS's AI/ML capabilities to design and deploy innovative solutions from startup to enterprise scale. Vasquez has been instrumental in shaping AWS's approach to delivering world-class solutions globally since joining in 2017.Kelly Day, Vice President, Prime Video InternationalKelly Day leads Prime Video’s international business, overseeing teams across 20+ countries who are focused on making Prime Video the first-stop entertainment destination for customers worldwide. Her role expanded in 2024 to include MGM+ and MGM Scripted Television teams. Day’s strategy integrates content, marketing, and partnerships to enhance the value Prime Video delivers to over 200 million Prime members globally.Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President, Public PolicyAshleigh de la Torre leads a Public Policy team supporting Amazon’s U.S. operations, logistics, and delivery businesses, and Amazon’s sustainability efforts. She and her team engage lawmakers and regulators at the local, state and federal levels on behalf of the company and our customers. Her work includes policymaker advocacy to improve transportation safety, support continued investment in transportation infrastructure, and encourage development of innovative and sustainable new technologies and delivery solutions.Rick Freeman, Vice President, Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS)With over 20 years of experience in defense and space industries, Rick oversees Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS), which will provide secure broadband services and space network solutions for government customers worldwide. KGS is the government-focused division of Project Kuiper, an Amazon initiative to deliver fast, reliable, and secure connectivity through a constellation of satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO). As a U.S. Marine Corps veteran, he is passionate about providing reliable satellite communications for mission-critical applications.Eric Orme, Vice President, Live Sports Product and Engineering, Prime VideoEric Orme has played a pivotal role in developing Prime Video’s live sports and entertainment strategy. His team delivers high-quality live experiences for global audiences, including events like the English Premier League and Thursday Night Football. Orme focuses on personalizing the sports viewing experience while ensuring the successful operation of every live event.Christine Enemark, VP & AGC, Prime VideoOver 13 years ago, when Christine joined Amazon, there were 3 lawyers supporting a new video streaming business operating in the United States. Today, she leads a global legal team of over 85 professionals across 14 countries, supporting Prime Video’s expansion into movies, TV shows, Amazon Originals, unscripted content, and live sports. She encourages her team to advise with the end customer in mind, shaping a legal strategy that aligns with Amazon’s customer-first approach. This perspective has strengthened both the team and the business.Jay Shankar, Vice President, Global Talent AcquisitionJay Shankar combines her engineering background with a customer-centric approach to recruiting. Since joining Amazon, she has led talent acquisition for key businesses like AWS, Advertising, and Prime Video. Shankar’s focus is on leveraging technology and data analytics to streamline recruiting processes and build a robust global talent pipeline. She also prioritizes inclusivity and career development, ensuring Amazon remains a leader in attracting top talent. Love is in the air: Celebrate Valentine’s Day with Amazon HQ2 at Met Park! This Valentine’s Day, Amazon HQ2 is teaming up with local favorites like Calluna Flower Truck, The Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA), Brightly Ever After, and a variety of Met Park restaurants for a festive celebration of love and community in Arlington, Virginia. As a proud neighbor, we're excited to bring everyone together with family-friendly events that celebrate the spirit of the season.Free roses and cardmaking at MoCA: MoCA is spreading the love with special giveaways and activities! From 11 a.m. ET to 2 p.m. ET, Calluna Flower Truck will hand out 500 free roses outside MoCA to both Amazonians and community members. If you're looking for the perfect gift, beautiful bouquets will also be available for purchase. After picking up your rose, head inside MoCA for a fun Cardmaking Workshop. Their team will help you craft heartfelt notes for your loved ones.Food pop-ups at Met Park: No celebration is complete without great food! On February 14, we’ll have a variety of delicious restaurant pop-ups at Met Park. Whether you're out with a friend or a special someone, come sample tasty treats and snacks to add flavor to your day.Valentine’s Day decorations at Met Park: To set the mood, we’re giving Met Park a romantic makeover with Valentine’s-themed sidewalk details and stunning floral decorations. Thanks to Brightly Ever After, the Pergolas near the dog park will be beautifully adorned, making it the perfect spot for a stroll or a photo op. Keep an eye out for vibrant decals leading you to the “I Heart Met Park” campaign page!Spread the love! Amazon HQ2 is committed to fostering creativity, connection, and community in Arlington. By partnering with local businesses like Calluna Flower Truck and Brightly Ever After, we’re helping make Met Park a place where everyone can come together and celebrate. We can't wait to celebrate Valentine’s Day with you—see you at Met Park! Celebrating minority entrepreneurs at Amazon’s HQ2 January 29th marks Minority Entrepreneur Day, a time to honor the contributions of minority-owned businesses. HQ2’s retailers are transforming the culinary landscape with unique flavors and cultural pride.Peruvian Brothers: Founded by brothers Giuseppe and Mario Lanzone, Peruvian Brothers serves bold, authentic Peruvian cuisine. From their food truck beginnings to a popular HQ2 restaurant, their success is built on fresh ingredients, tradition, and community connection.“From Day 1 twelve years ago, our motto has been, “we’re more than just a food truck; it’s about creating a community where our staff knows people by name, remembers their favorite orders, and shares the best parts of our Peruvian cuisine and culture through experiences like Alpaca Happy Hours.” — Giuseppe Lanzone, Co-Founder & Chief Operating OfficerToby’s is known for ice cream with a modern twist, blending international flavors into a locally-sourced menu. Their commitment to quality and community has made it a local favorite and a symbol of perseverance.“As a minority business owner, sometimes I feel we are overlooked by the community. Amazon has been very supportive every step of the way, and takes every opportunity to include us in their events. We are looking forward to a long, fruitful partnership and prosperous 2025.” Toby Bantug, OwnerTaqueria Xochi specializes in authentic Mexican street food, from tacos to tamales. Xochi’s passion for tradition and innovation makes this spot a hub for Mexican culture and hospitality.“We’re thrilled to bring the vibrant, authentic flavors of Mexican cuisine to Amazon HQ2. Beyond serving great food and drinks, our goal is to provide a welcoming space where employees and the local community can gather, connect, and feel truly at home.” — Geraldine Mendoza, Director of Operations & PartnerMakers Union offers a fun menu perfect for any occasion, including pet-friendly patio lunches. As part of one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food companies, it thrives in the HQ2 community.“Our fun and approachable menu is perfect for your next happy hour, business dinner, or lunch with your pup on the pet-friendly patio. Makers Union is proudly owned and operated by Thompson Restaurants, one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food and facilities management companies.” — Warren Thompson, President and Chairman A year in review at HQ2: Continuing to deliver for our communities 2024 was an exciting year for us and our partners in the National Capital Region. As we celebrate the new year, we want to take a moment to reflect on our collective impact, express gratitude to our neighbors and community partners who continue to welcome us with open arms, and acknowledge the work of so many Amazon employees who are working hard to serve our customers and communities.In 2024, we welcomed three new small businesses, and we now have 14 retailers open at Met Park—most of which are women and minority-owned. Local businesses are already experiencing a 13% increase in foot traffic and 1 million additional visits to the region since HQ2 opened its doors, and we’re excited for this growth to continue as Amazon employees work from the office more frequently in 2025.We remain deeply committed to the National Capital Region, donating more than $78 million to community groups and nonprofits in 2024 alone. As a result, we were honored to be named the top corporate philanthropist for the second year in a row by the Washington Business Journal.We were also proud to receive LEED Platinum v4 certification from the U.S. Green Building Council, making Amazon HQ2 the largest newly constructed building in the world to achieve this distinction. This milestone reinforces our commitment to sustainability, while representing a meaningful step towards decarbonizing Amazon’s real estate portfolio in order to meet our Climate Pledge commitment.As we enter the new year, we’re inspired by the innovation, collaboration, and connection that we’ve already seen with our teams working in person together. With most employees working from the office each day, we look forward to continuing to support local small businesses while contributing to economic growth and the overall vibrancy of National Landing.We are incredibly proud of everything our teammates are accomplishing, as well as our ongoing commitment to our customers, employees, and the communities we call home. Here's to an exciting 2025 and beyond—Amazon will continue to strive to be a good neighbor and trusted business partner in the region. BEN-DMV hosts third annual 'Great Big Kid' event, celebrating largest Amazon-Boys & Girls Club partnership Amazon's Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) recently celebrated a monumental milestone in its ongoing partnership with the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington, hosting the third annual \"Great Big Kid\" event at Amazon's HQ2. This year’s celebration was the largest and most impactful to date, highlighting the continued growth and success of this powerful initiative.The “Great Big Kid” program, a six-week mentorship initiative, pairs Amazon volunteers with local youth, providing them with invaluable opportunities for personal and professional growth. This year’s event saw over 200 attendees, including mentees, mentors, families, and Amazon leadership, all gathering at HQ2 to commemorate the program’s extraordinary achievements.“What started as a simple sponsorship has grown into a dynamic, multi-faceted partnership,” said Cash Ashley, President of BEN-DMV. “We’ve gone from having just one mentor in the program to hosting field trips, providing multiple mentors, and even welcoming students to our AWS Skills Center.”The event featured an inspiring keynote from Keyon Young, Director at Amazon, who shared his perspective on the power of mentorship. Drawing from his experiences as an executive director in Albany, NY, and his current role at Amazon, Keyon emphasized how mentorship profoundly impacts both mentors and mentees. Building on this theme, Gabrielle Webster, CEO of Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington, spoke to the transformative nature of the partnership, stating, \"This collaboration with Amazon’s Black Employee Network goes beyond funding. It’s about building lasting connections and creating meaningful opportunities for our youth.\"The partnership’s impact reaches far beyond the annual event. Two Amazonians now hold board seats across different Boys & Girls Club regions, reinforcing the deepening connection between the organizations and ensuring continued support for the next generation.By the Numbers:3 years of partnership$155,000 total investment over three years7 Amazon mentors in this year’s program2 field trips to AWS Skills Center200+ attendees at the HQ2 celebration26 Boys & Girls Club sites represented12,000 youth impacted across the DMV areaThis event highlights the impact we can achieve as a community. The passion of our affinity group and the dedication of volunteers like Quanteer have driven three years of success. Our collaboration shows that by uniting for a common cause, we can create lasting change and opportunities for youth in need. Amazon's HQ2 Community Day: celebrating one year of partnership and fun at National Landing This fall, Amazon hosted HQ2’s Community Day, a celebration commemorating HQ2’s one-year anniversary since opening its doors to employees and the community in National Landing.HQ2 Community Day commemorated Amazon’s ongoing dedication to being a good neighbor and community partner in the region. Since announcing plans to build its second headquarters in Arlington, Amazon has worked each day to fulfill its commitment to becoming one of the region's most trusted business and community partners. Last year, Amazon donated more than $78 million to over 100 organizations in the area, making it the top corporate philanthropist in the region for the second consecutive year.At Community Day, more than 1,000 neighbors of all ages gathered in Met Park to enjoy a variety of activities. This included a “Taste of Met Park” that highlighted the small businesses and restaurants that call Met Park home. Other activities included a community library, law games, a Lego wall, face painting, and more. With over 50 local vendors, Met Park's Saturday Market offered produce, hot food and handcrafted art. A photo booth with themed props and a live DJ kept the energy lively throughout the day. Amazon launches high school Apprenticeship Program for D.C. and Virginia students in partnership with CityWor We are proud to welcome 21 high school students from Arlington County and D.C. to Amazon’s JumpStart Youth Apprenticeship Program in partnership with CityWorks DC, an affiliate of CareerWiseUSA. These students will work in a variety of technical and business operations roles under the guidance of several teams at Amazon’s HQ2, including Amazon Web Services, Prime Video, Finance, and Generative AI.“This region’s dedication to education and workforce training were a significant factor in our decision to select Arlington for HQ2,” said Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s Vice President of Worldwide Economic Development. “We recognize that work-based learning programs are an important component for future academic and career success, and to help close the opportunity gap for the underrepresented and underserved. We are thrilled to welcome these incredible student leaders to Amazon.”The two-year program for rising high school juniors and seniors is designed to be an options multiplier for students—accelerating both their future academic and career prospects. Throughout JumpStart, apprentices complete an 11-week cohort-based onboarding program followed by paid part-time work at Amazon as a complement to their high school coursework.“We know that talent is equally distributed, but opportunity is not,” said Jennifer C. Niles, President and CEO of CityWorks DC. “Creating a direct pathway into Amazon from our public high schools is the bold action we need to truly create a local talent pipeline into high-wage, high-demand careers. We have the talent and JumpStart is the opportunity.\"\"We are proud of our students and look forward to seeing the positive impact this experience will have on their future academic and career trajectories,” said Dr. Francisco Durán, Superintendent, Arlington Public Schools. “JumpStart is an extraordinary opportunity for our students to gain new skills working alongside professionals at one of the world’s leading companies. We are grateful for Amazon's commitment to education and look forward to seeing all our students will achieve.”“Meaningful, cross-sector collaborations like JumpStart bridge critical gaps between education systems and the workforce, and apprenticeships provide invaluable opportunities to shape the career paths and futures of our students,” said DC Public Schools Chancellor, Dr. Lewis D. Ferebee. “We know these experiences not only equip our young people with essential skills, but they also enhance their mobility and prepare them for success well beyond their time with DC Public Schools.\"We are excited to partner with Amazon and CityWorks DC to provide our students with real-world training in high-demand fields while still in high school,” said Patricia Brantley, CEO of Friendship Public Charter Schools. “Through this apprenticeship, Amazon will help our students gain valuable skills, earn a competitive wage, and build meaningful pathways to college and careers. At Friendship Public Charter School, we are committed to providing every opportunity for our students' success. Amazon is making that possible. We look forward to seeing our students thrive and make a positive impact at Amazon and beyond.\"Amazon’s JumpStart program first began in New York in 2019. Nearly 200 NYC students have participated in the program and are now pursuing college coursework and/or working in roles at Amazon, including in software development. Learn more about a recent Amazon JumpStart youth apprentice and Amazon UX Designer. Amazon showcases commitment to National Capital Region in latest Community Impact Report At Amazon, we live our mission of making our customers’ lives better and easier every day. We also bring this innovative, inventive spirit to empower the communities we call home, using our scale and abilities to take on some of society’s most pressing challenges. Working side-by-side with community partners, we’re increasing access to affordable housing and alleviating hunger. We’re strengthening education, promoting literacy, and helping people gain skills for the future.Our most recent Community Impact Report showcases the progress we’re making and the positive impact we believe we’re having on everyday life for our National Capital Region neighbors. Highlights include:We contributed $78 million last year in support of more than 90 community partners dedicated to making the National Capital Region a better place to live. Through initiatives large and small, our partners’ work has an indelible impact—providing essential social services to those who need them most, ensuring young people have access to learning opportunities, revitalizing our parks and green spaces, and continuing beloved area traditions like the National Cherry Blossom Festival and the Arlington County Fair.Since launching the Amazon Housing Equity Fund in 2021, we have committed over $1.2 billion to help create or preserve affordable housing for more than 20,700 low- to moderate-income individuals so they can live in the communities they serve and love. This is especially important for first responders, teachers, nurses, and service industry employees who are getting priced out of expensive metro areas like ours.We’ve donated more than $2.7 million since 2021 to local organizations tackling food insecurity and hunger across the National Capital Region. Our support recently helped the Capital Area Food Bank build its new warehouse in Lorton, Virginia, enabling the distribution of 13 million meals annually to Northern Virginia residents experiencing food insecurity.We continue to invest in programs to help children in grades K-12, higher-education students, and adult learners unlock their potential. By focusing on critical digital skills, we prepare these individuals for the jobs of the future. Through Amazon Future Engineer— our childhood-to-career computer science program—we delivered 74,000 student hours of STEM education, literacy, and career exploration courses across the National Capital Region. And over 55,000 learners have accessed free training and education at the AWS Skills Center in Arlington.These are just a few of the ways that we are giving back to the region that’s become our second home. We are committed to supporting and collaborating with our neighbors and communities throughout the National Capital Region. We know that—working together—we can build an even brighter future.Learn more about what we’re doing for our employees, neighbors, and the community.Explore our Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region. Operation Green Light at HQ2: A powerful partnership to honor veterans Amazon's HQ2 is glowing a vivid green again in observance of veterans of the armed forces. Starting on November 4 and running through November 11, 2024, this inspirational display symbolizes Amazon's ongoing support of Operation Green Light for Veterans. In partnership with the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO), this nationwide initiative aims to highlight the resources available to support veterans and their families while bringing attention to the significant challenges they face.Why Green?The color green is a universal representation of honor, rebirth, and unwavering courage. Lighting up our headquarters in this empowering color not only honors Veterans Day but also visually affirms our support and raises public awareness of our veterans' ongoing needs.A Global CommitmentAmazon is committed to this cause, even beyond the image. More than 150 of Amazon’s operations locations across the country are participating in this powerful and luminary initiative. Veterans Day, observed annually on November 11, invites everyone to reflect on the bravery and sacrifices made by veterans. Amazon is proud to play a role in this reflection and acknowledgment, ensuring that the valor and hardships of veterans remain at the forefront of our national conversation.\"As we light over 150 operations sites and buildings across the country for Operation Green Light, we are proud to continue our partnership with the National Association of Counties and encourage organizations across the country to join us in support of and celebration of our veterans,” said Charlotte LaBelle, Leader of Amazon’s Global Military Affairs and Army veteran. “At Amazon, we value the unique skills and experience that the military community brings to our company. This year we surpassed our goal of hiring 100,000 veterans and military spouses and are committed to continue supporting our military-affiliated community and to helping veterans transition into roles in the private sector.”Throughout the week, the green glow will represent appreciation and solidarity for residents of Arlington County, Virginia, and other participating communities. It is a simple but powerful reminder of our support to those who have served and protected us while in uniform.Thank YouOur special thanks goes out to our Warriors@Amazon affinity group, who have worked tirelessly to ensure that our military community is acknowledged. “With more than 600 chapters and 20,000 members globally, we rely on our chapter leaders to recognize this community and develop resources for transitioning veterans. We appreciate everyone who continues to stand by those who have served and are currently serving,” says Michelle Marchiano, President of Warriors@Amazon. HQ2 prepares to welcome Arlington Community High School to Metropolitan Park in 2026 As part of our commitment to providing a home for Arlington Community High School (ACHS) by 2026, we are finalizing plans to welcome more than 250 ACHS students, faculty, and staff to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park in time for the 2026-2027 school year. Since we selected Arlington as the home of our second headquarters in 2018, we have worked closely with the county and School Board to address one of the community’s most pressing issues—finding a location for ACHS which has never had a permanent home.“We look forward to this bright and exciting new chapter for Arlington Community High School and our students and staff. Having a new dedicated learning space, custom-built to serve their needs in an accessible location, will make a world of difference for our students as they pursue their academic and professional goals,” said Dr. Francisco Durán, superintendent, Arlington Public Schools. “We thank Amazon for their ongoing partnership in serving our students and commitment toward advancing equity and education in Arlington.”“Arlington Community High School plays a vital role in the educational and career development of these 250 students and we’re thrilled to welcome them to our second headquarters in 2026,” said Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s vice president of worldwide economic development. “By providing a new location for ACHS, we’re able to help deliver on our commitment to the Arlington community, while helping to unlock new economic and job opportunities for our neighbors, today and in the future.”Arlington Community High School serves a diverse student body of students ages 16 and older from across Arlington County, many of whom work full- or part-time jobs to support their families as they earn their high school diplomas. In addition to dedicated learning space at HQ2, students will have access to an adjacent 2.5 acre public park, community garden, dog run, and 14 local, small businesses.Amazon’s partnership with ACHS is an important piece of the company’s broad commitment to being a good neighbor and trusted community partner. We have continued to focus our effort, time, and resources on partnering with nonprofits and community groups to address challenges like equity in education, sustainability, and affordable housing. To date, we have donated more than $239 million to local community organizations and our commitment to providing a home for ACHS will also reduce the long-term operating and capital cost for Arlington Public Schools. Amazon Fresh cultivates healthy eating habits and educational opportunities in the Washington D.C. metro regi Amazon Fresh has teamed up with Whole Foods Market Foundation by donating $250,000 to fund 83 school gardens across the country, including local schools across the Washington D.C. metro region. This initiative aligns with Amazon Fresh's long-term vision of positively impacting local communities by promoting healthy eating habits and educational initiatives.Among the beneficiaries are Montessori Public School of Arlington, Dr. Charles R. Drew Elementary, Oakridge Elementary, and Gunston Middle School in Arlington, as well as Herndon High in Herndon, Virginia. Hillcrest Heights ES Planet Protectors in Temple Hill, Capital City Lower Public Charter School in Washington, Takoma Park Elementary in Takoma Park, East Silver Spring Elementary School, and New Hampshire Estates Elementary School in Silver Spring will also receive Whole Kids Garden Grants. These grants will enable the schools to establish or enhance their edible gardens and engage students in hands-on learning about growing and enjoying fresh produce.\"We're thrilled to support these local schools and their efforts to cultivate a love for nutritious foods among students,\" said Jim Gillespie, regional manager for Amazon Fresh in the HQ2 region. \"By providing access to edible gardens, we aim to inspire a new generation of kids to develop an appreciation for where their food comes from.\"\"When kids have a hand in growing fruits and vegetables, they are more likely to try them, enjoy them, and inspire other kids to do the same. Additionally, kids who grow up enjoying fruits and vegetables are more likely to continue eating them through adulthood,” said Carol Medeiros, executive director of Whole Foods Market Foundation. “We’re so grateful to Amazon Fresh whose generous donation in the Whole Kids Garden Grant program this year will allow more students to get a jumpstart on a lifetime of enjoying healthy food.\"Through this collaboration, Amazon Fresh reinforces its commitment to investing in the well-being of local communities in the HQ2 region. By nurturing a love for fresh produce and sustainable practices from an early age, the company hopes to inspire positive change and contribute to a healthier, more sustainable future for all. Highlights from Amazon’s Affordable Housing Summit at HQ2 When Amazon chose Arlington as the home of HQ2, we made a commitment to be a trusted business and community partner in the region. The work to become a part of the fabric of this community reflects that commitment daily. We partner with many community leaders and organizations to ensure we’re making the most impactful contributions—our Housing Equity Fund (HEF) is a powerful example of how we can address community needs through innovative partnerships. In an effort to bring together and celebrate our community and public-sector partners, Amazon recently hosted an Affordable Housing Summit at HQ2's Amazon Visitor Landing. The summit gathered policymakers, affordable housing partners, and community leaders for a discussion about housing challenges, and how we can work together to identify and implement solutions. The event came on the heels of Amazon’s announcement that the company’s HEF would extend its commitment to include an additional $1.4 billion to create and preserve an additional 14,000 homes in our hometown communities, including Arlington.Since the HEF launched in 2021, positive impacts in the HQ2 region have been significant. To date, we have:Committed $1.1 billion to help create and preserve more than 9,400 affordable homes across the National Capital regionAmazon’s funding has helped increase affordable housing by 23% in Arlington, VA58% of Amazon-funded affordable homes across the region will serve households earning less than 60% of the area median income (AMI)More than 80 community leaders and affordable housing advocates attended the Affordable Housing Summit, which included a fireside chat with Senator Mark Warner (VA) and a panel discussion with Amazon Housing Equity Fund’s Managing Principal Senthil Sankaran, Arlington Partnership for Affordable Housing President Carmen Romero, and LEO Impact Capital President Brian Allan “AJ” Jackson. Participants focused on the importance of public-private partnerships to make a meaningful difference for thousands of families across Virginia. Both discussions were moderated by WTOP’s Capitol Hill Correspondent, Mitchell Miller. Explore the Met Park Butterfly Garden at Amazon’s HQ2 Met Park is home to many things—over a dozen women- and minority-owned small businesses, works of art that honor the National Capital Region, world-leading sustainable buildings, and more. Now it’s home to butterflies, too.Met Park’s Butterfly Garden opened on June 20 to a crowd of Amazon employees and their families, children from the Celebree School of National Landing, and members of the community, who all showed up to see these little natural wonders. The Butterfly Garden is a deeply precious community attraction that sits at the crossing point of two of Amazon’s priorities: being a leader in sustainability and being a good neighbor to the Arlington community.“Our 2.5-acre public park is home to a diverse range of native plants that both beautify the surrounding area and nourish the soil in which they are planted,” said Lead Horticulturist Dillan Clark. “Once we realized that our park grounds already included many of the attributes and native plants that are needed to foster a home for butterflies, we partnered with the North American Butterfly Association to explore an expansion of Amazon’s horticultural attractions and bring the butterfly garden to life.”Met Park was planned with native and adaptive plantings that are conducive to the Arlington climate—the soil is engineered to retain water and promote root growth while smart and low-flow irrigation systems are optimized to reduce water consumption by providing only what the native plants need. The garden currently includes ten Painted Lady butterflies that are currently in their chrysalises metamorphosing from caterpillar into butterfly—but they’ll be spreading their wings soon.Amazon’s second headquarters was built with sustainability at its core, including two acres (or nearly 100,000 square feet) of green roofs with native plantings where visitors and employees alike can find respite and connect to nature. The Amazon Horticulture team leads a program in partnership with the local Love and Carrots organization, grows fresh produce that gets delivered to the local nonprofit Kitchen of Purpose, with 800 pounds of produce contributing to over 600 meals per week.Thank you to the horticulturists, volunteers, and employees, who have been pivotal in the launch of Met Park’s Butterfly Garden. Amazon adds $1.4 billion to the Housing Equity Fund to create and preserve more affordable homes in the Natio We’re now extending our commitment to the Amazon Housing Equity Fund with an additional $1.4 billion in funding to create and preserve 14,000 more homes in three of its hometown communities—Washington State's Puget Sound region; the Arlington, Virginia/National Capital region; and Nashville, Tennessee. This brings the total to more than $3.6 billion to fund more than 35,000 affordable homes across these three regions. The extension of the Housing Equity Fund underscores our commitment to affordable housing and to helping thousands of families live closer to where they work or near transportation hubs, removing a major barrier to success. To date, Amazon's Housing Equity Fund has helped create and preserve 9,500+ homes across the National Capital region and increased Arlington, VA’s affordable housing stock by 23%.Learn more about this extension of the Housing Equity Fund and how the Amazon Housing Equity Fund is helping create affordable housing across communities that Amazon calls home. HQ2 receives Urban Land Institute Washington’s top recognition for Excellence in Sustainability Amazon’s Metropolitan Park at HQ2 was recently recognized as Urban Land Institute (ULI) Washington’s Excellence in Sustainability recipient during their 2024 awards for excellence. The award recognized HQ2 as a project that made outstanding contributions to development within the D.C. region, and one that exemplifies innovation and creativity that inspires vibrant communities.HQ2 was built with sustainability at its core, and we are proud that Met Park’s towers are the largest buildings in the world to be LEED Platinum v4 certified by the US Green Building Council. Met Park is all electric and powered by 100% renewable energy—including each of the 13 retailers on our ground floor. Sustainability-focused initiatives are prevalent throughout Met Park, including lower-carbon concrete, mass timber, advanced ways to reuse water, and even bird-friendly glass to protect wildlife on their important migratory path.Amazon congratulates fellow ULI award recipients who are working to collectively drive transformative change across the region.Learn more about HQ2’s sustainability-focused construction and design. Isabel Guzman, Administrator of the U.S. Small Business Administration, visits Amazon's HQ2 retailers Amazon’s vision for its HQ2 has been to bring together employees, local residents, and visitors to enjoy a vibrant, 18-hour neighborhood. We are proud to offer more than 50,000 square feet of retail space for 13 small businesses at HQ2’s Metropolitan Park, many of which are minority and women-owned.We were honored to welcome the U.S. Small Business Administration’s Administrator Isabel Guzman to HQ2 to meet several of the business owners and employees who are part of our retail community. Administrator Guzman serves in President Biden’s Cabinet as the leading advocate for America’s 33 million small businesses and innovative startups. Watch the recap of Administrator Guzman’s visit. Museum of Contemporary Art (MoCA) Arlington studio and store now open at Amazon HQ2 The Innovation Studio + Store showcases contemporary art and adds to a growing list of vibrant local retailers at Metropolitan Park.Amazon is proud to welcome the Museum of Contemporary Art (MoCA) Arlington to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park. The new Innovation Studio + Store is a place for Arlington residents and visitors to learn more about contemporary art, interact with professional artists at work, participate in free public art and artist programs, explore their creativity in private classes, and shop various creative and inspiring products from the museum store.“Community is at the heart of everything we do here at Amazon. We are honored that the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington is joining HQ2 to enrich the Arlington community by connecting residents and visitors to exhibitions, educational programming, and artist residencies,” said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement at Amazon. “This is a great new chapter for all of us who live, work, and play in National Landing.”“We are thrilled to open our new Innovation Studio + Store at Amazon HQ2,” said Catherine Anchin, MoCA Arlington’s executive director. “We know that contemporary art can feel intimidating to some. This space was intentionally designed to feel warm and inviting, with the hope that people will be inspired to join us for a class, participate in a workshop, or simply support local artists.”Since coming to National Landing, Amazon has prioritized providing a home for small, local businesses, with a focus on those which are women- and minority-owned. See a full list of local retailers at HQ2’s Met Park. Amazon details community investments in newly released Community Impact Report for the National Capital Regio Nearly one year after opening the doors to HQ2’s Metropolitan Park, Amazon recaps its community engagement activities and investments in the region.We are proud to work with nonprofits, schools, and community leaders across the National Capital region to support equity in education, affordable housing development, and regional, inclusive economic growth.We recently released Amazon’s updated Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region, where we shared details about how we’re delivering for our customers, employees, and the communities where we operate. Success and scale bring broad responsibility and as we continue to grow, we are committed to using our resources to help address important challenges that our hometown communities face.Here are a few highlights of our community engagement efforts and positive impact over the past year: We worked with more than 100 nonprofits in and around Arlington, Virginia, which is home to Amazon’s second headquarters (HQ2). Our support went to organizations like Bridges to Independence, which is working to break intergenerational cycles of poverty; the English Empowerment Center, which serves nearly 1,500 beginning-level English language and literacy adult learners; and Aspire, which helps historically underserved young people fulfill their potential through afterschool and summer learning programs that support and connect families, schools, and communities.Amazon was named the top philanthropic company in the Washington, D.C., metro area by the Washington Business Journal. Since 2018, Amazon has invested more than $161 million in local nonprofits, community groups, organizations, businesses, and public schools.Amazon launched a new housing pilot initiative that will help nearly 1,000 individuals and families from underserved communities become homeowners in Virginia and Washington, D.C., as well as Washington state and Tennessee. This is part of a broader set of initiatives from the Amazon Housing Equity Fund. Since 2021, Amazon has committed over $1 billion in loans and grants to create or preserve more than 7,500 affordable housing units in and around the National Capital Region.Amazon continued to advance its commitment to equity in education through several partnerships and funding efforts, including Amazon Future Engineer, a comprehensive childhood-to-career program aimed at increasing access to computer science education for students from underserved and underrepresented communities. Amazon helped students broaden their horizons through education via:$1.8 million in scholarships awarded to 45 Capital Region students by the Amazon Future Engineer program241 schools and 23,470 students across the Capital Region received STEM education and career exploration courses13,000+ students have access to three AWS Think Big Spaces in VirginiaAmazon has continued to support and integrate local businesses into its Arlington headquarters, representing vibrant local favorites, women- and minority-owned retailers, and others. For example, visitors can get their bike serviced at Conte’s Bike Shop, drop off furry friends at doggy daycare at District Dogs, get a workout in at HUSTLE, grab a bite to eat at Peruvian Brothers, and so much more.Amazon will continue to strive to be a good neighbor and trusted business partner across the region.Learn more about what we’re doing for our employees, neighbors, and the community.Read more in our Community Impact Report for the National Capital Region.
